descripcion: The Ocean Extra plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via custom fields in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor access or higher, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

Qiskit IBM Runtime is an environment that streamlines quantum computations and provides optimal implementations of the Qiskit quantum computing SDK. Starting in version 0.1.0 and prior to version 0.21.2, deserializing json data using `qiskit_ibm_runtime.RuntimeDecoder` can lead to arbitrary code execution given a correctly formatted input string. Version 0.21.2 contains a fix for this issue.

The dormakaba Saflok system before the November 2023 software update allows an attacker to unlock arbitrary doors at a property via forged keycards, if the attacker has obtained one active or expired keycard for the specific property, aka the "Unsaflok" issue. This occurs, in part, because the key derivation function relies only on a UID. This affects, for example, Saflok MT, and the Confidant, Quantum, RT, and Saffire series.

Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Quantum Cloud Slider Hero allows Stored XSS.This issue affects Slider Hero: from n/a through 8.6.1.

liboqs is a C-language cryptographic library that provides implementations of post-quantum cryptography algorithms. A control-flow timing lean has been identified in the reference implementation of the Kyber key encapsulation mechanism when it is compiled with Clang 15-18 for `-Os`, `-O1`, and other compilation options. A proof-of-concept local attack on the reference implementation leaks the entire ML-KEM 512 secret key in ~10 minutes using end-to-end decapsulation timing measurements. The issue has been fixed in version 0.10.1. As a possible workaround, some compiler options may produce vectorized code that does not leak secret information, however relying on these compiler options as a workaround may not be reliable.

oqs-provider is a provider for the OpenSSL 3 cryptography library that adds support for post-quantum cryptography in TLS, X.509, and S/MIME using post-quantum algorithms from liboqs. Flaws have been identified in the way oqs-provider handles lengths decoded with DECODE_UINT32 at the start of serialized hybrid (traditional + post-quantum) keys and signatures. Unchecked length values are later used for memory reads and writes; malformed input can lead to crashes or information leakage. Handling of plain/non-hybrid PQ key operation is not affected. This issue has been patched in in v0.6.1. All users are advised to upgrade. There are no workarounds for this issue.

Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in OceanWP Ocean Extra allows Stored XSS.This issue affects Ocean Extra: from n/a through 2.2.9.

The Ocean Extra plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the ‘twitter_username’ parameter in versions up to, and including, 2.2.6 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level permissions and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

liboqs is a C-language cryptographic library that provides implementations of post-quantum cryptography algorithms. A correctness error has been identified in the reference implementation of the HQC key encapsulation mechanism. Due to an indexing error, part of the secret key is incorrectly treated as non-secret data. This results in an incorrect shared secret value being returned when the decapsulation function is called with a malformed ciphertext. This vulnerability is fixed in 0.12.0.

Improper access control vulnerability in Prodys' Quantum Audio codec affecting versions 2.3.4t and below. This vulnerability could allow an unauthenticated user to bypass authentication entirely and execute arbitrary API requests against the web application.

The Ocean Extra plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the Flickr widget in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.8 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.
In Ocean Data Systems Dream Report, a path traversal vulnerability could allow an attacker to perform remote code execution through the injection of a malicious dynamic-link library (DLL).
In Ocean Data Systems Dream Report, an incorrect permission vulnerability could allow a local unprivileged attacker to escalate their privileges and could cause a denial-of-service.

title: CP2K_USE_PW_GPU broken on AMD GPUs with > 1 MPI rank per device
description:
When running CP2K on AMD GPUs (Mi250x and Mi300a) with multiple MPI ranks per GPU, the numerics are not guaranteed to be correct. The symptoms are inconsistent total energies, and sometimes Cholesky decomposition failure. This happens with well tested input files taken from the `benchmarks` directory of CP2K.

This issue does not occur when running a single MPI rank per GPU. I have narrowed it down to the `CP2K_USE_PW_GPU` compilation flag. When compiling without it, CP2K behaves consistently and correctly again.

title: Units of Wannier spreading
description:
Dear CP2K community,

This question might be a duplicate of the discussion here: https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/gx-_qPP2Vdw.

Based on Prof. Hutter's @juerghutter response in that thread (and the code snippet he provided) as well as the equation from https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevB.61.10040, the spread appears to be unitless and needs to be rescaled by the box length. 
However, when I tried varying the cell size, the spread did not change.

Additionally, I tried with both ang and Bohr units and compared the spread to the electron density, but neither of them seems to match. I suspect there is some constant or scaling factor I might be overlooking.

Could anyone provide clarification or point me in the right direction?

description:
I tried to run simulations with i-PI and using CP2K as the force client and ran into problems with the newest versions. For 2024.1, I tried both a local cluster parallel build module and the available precompiled binary, for 2025.1 only the precompiled binary. All lead to the same error
`Error opening UNIX socket: path unavailable, or already existing: No such file or directory`; running the same input files with CP2K version 9.1 works smoothly.

I put together a MWE of a single water molecule simulation at a DFTB level that reproduces the error. The CP2K input is as follows:
```
&GLOBAL
  PROJECT water
  PREFERRED_FFT_LIBRARY FFTW
  FFTW_PLAN_TYPE MEASURE
  RUN_TYPE DRIVER
  PRINT_LEVEL LOW
&END GLOBAL

&FORCE_EVAL
  &DFT
    CHARGE 0
    &QS
      METHOD DFTB
      &DFTB
        DIAGONAL_DFTB3
        HB_SR_GAMMA
        &PARAMETER
          PARAM_FILE_PATH 3ob-3-1
          PARAM_FILE_NAME scc_parameter
          HB_SR_PARAM 4.0
        &END PARAMETER
      &END DFTB
      EXTRAPOLATION_ORDER 3
    &END QS
    &SCF
      MAX_SCF 200
      EPS_SCF 1.0E-7
      SCF_GUESS ATOMIC
      &MIXING
      &END
      &PRINT
        &RESTART OFF
        &END RESTART
      &END PRINT
    &END SCF
    &POISSON
      PERIODIC NONE
      POISSON_SOLVER WAVELET
    &END POISSON
  &END DFT
  &SUBSYS
    &TOPOLOGY
      COORD_FILE_NAME pos.xyz
      COORD_FILE_FORMAT XYZ
    &END TOPOLOGY
    &CELL
      ABC 50.0 50.0 50.0
      PERIODIC NONE
    &END CELL
    &KIND H
      DFTB3_PARAM -0.1857
    &END KIND
    &KIND O
      DFTB3_PARAM -0.1575
    &END KIND
  &END SUBSYS
&END FORCE_EVAL

&MOTION
  &DRIVER
    UNIX TRUE
    HOST myunixsocket
    PORT 31415
  &END DRIVER
&END MOTION

title: cp_fm_struct - Fix for small matrices
description:
## Introduction

I initially observed that regtests in `QS/regtest-scalable-gw` started to fail on my local machine after commit e7c81ba798 . I recognized that I run the regtests with 4 mpiranks and 1 ompthread, while the CI runs the tests with 2 mpiranks and 2 ompthread. After trying to run the regtests on local machine again with 2x2 (mpiranks x ompthreads), the test succeeded, so I recognized that the distribution of some tasks to the mpiranks probably contains an error.

## Error statement and fix

After consultation with @JWilhelm and @RemiPasquier , we deduced that the determination of the maximum number of rows/columns in a blacs block contains the source of the error - there was a change in this determination in e7c81ba798 which introduced this error. @RemiPasquier mentioned this on the pull request and I am now presenting the solution we arrived at independently. We believe that the maximum number of rows/columns in a blacs block should not be essentially the entire matrix, as this leads to some ranks containing zero local data. The following change restores previous behaviour (while hopefully still allowing for setting the block size to vector length vlen introduced in e7c81ba798)
```
diff --git a/src/fm/cp_fm_struct.F b/src/fm/cp_fm_struct.F
index 4e1cc2238e..ed67078ccd 100644
--- a/src/fm/cp_fm_struct.F
+++ b/src/fm/cp_fm_struct.F
@@ -205,7 +205,7 @@ CONTAINS
       IF (.NOT. my_force_block) THEN
          vlen = m_cpuid_vlen()
          nmax_block = (fmstruct%nrow_global + fmstruct%context%num_pe(1) - 1)/ &
-                      (fmstruct%context%num_pe(1))*fmstruct%context%num_pe(1)
+                      (fmstruct%context%num_pe(1))
          IF (1 < vlen) THEN ! flooring not ceiling (OOB)
             fmstruct%nrow_block = fmstruct%nrow_block/vlen*vlen
             nmax_block = nmax_block/vlen*vlen
@@ -213,7 +213,7 @@ CONTAINS
          fmstruct%nrow_block = MAX(MIN(fmstruct%nrow_block, nmax_block), 1)
 
          nmax_block = (fmstruct%ncol_global + fmstruct%context%num_pe(2) - 1)/ &
-                      (fmstruct%context%num_pe(1))*fmstruct%context%num_pe(2)
+                      (fmstruct%context%num_pe(2))
          IF (1 < vlen) THEN ! flooring not ceiling (OOB)
             fmstruct%ncol_block = fmstruct%ncol_block/vlen*vlen
             nmax_block = nmax_block/vlen*vlen

```

## Error showcase

To show a specific error case, feel free to apply the following patch to current master and run the above mentioned part of the regtests
```
diff --git a/src/gw_large_cell_gamma.F b/src/gw_large_cell_gamma.F
index 1f3dd7d334..ce3a460c01 100644
--- a/src/gw_large_cell_gamma.F
+++ b/src/gw_large_cell_gamma.F
@@ -65,7 +65,8 @@ MODULE gw_large_cell_gamma
                                               int_8
    USE kpoint_coulomb_2c,               ONLY: build_2c_coulomb_matrix_kp
    USE kpoint_types,                    ONLY: kpoint_type
-   USE machine,                         ONLY: m_walltime
+   USE machine,                         ONLY: m_walltime, &
+                                              m_cpuid_vlen
    USE mathconstants,                   ONLY: twopi,&
                                               z_one,&
                                               z_zero
@@ -2290,10 +2291,19 @@ CONTAINS
             ! 2. get S_µν(k_i) from S_µν(k=0)
             CALL cfm_ikp_from_fm_Gamma(cfm_s_ikp, bs_env%fm_s_Gamma, &
                                        ikp, qs_env, bs_env%kpoints_DOS, "ORB")
+            PRINT *, "cpuid_vlen", m_cpuid_vlen()
+            PRINT *, "cart. position", cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%context%mepos(1), &
+                    cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%context%mepos(2), &
+                    "local_data size", &
+                    cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%nrow_locals(cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%context%mepos(1)), &
+                    cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%ncol_locals(cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%context%mepos(2))
 
             ! 3. Diagonalize (Roothaan-Hall): H_KS(k_i)*C(k_i) = S(k_i)*C(k_i)*ϵ(k_i)
             CALL cp_cfm_geeig(cfm_ks_ikp, cfm_s_ikp, cfm_mos_ikp, &
                               bs_env%eigenval_scf(:, ikp, ispin), cfm_work_ikp)
+            PRINT *, cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%context%mepos(1), &
+                    cfm_ks_ikp%matrix_struct%context%mepos(2), &
+                    bs_env%eigenval_scf(:, ikp, ispin)
 
             ! 4. V^xc_µν(k=0) -> V^xc_µν(k_i) -> V^xc_nn(k_i)
             CALL to_ikp_and_mo(V_xc_ikp_n, bs_env%fm_V_xc_Gamma(ispin), &
```

(you can use `cd tests && ./do_regtest.py --mpiranks 4 --ompthreads 1 --workbasedir ../regtesting --restrictdir QS/regtest-scalable-gw` + cp2k architecture and version to run the regtest)

This patch prints the distribution of a complex Hamiltonian matrix used in the GW workflow, and the Hamiltonian eigenvalues. At the current master, the print produces something similar to this for `01_G0W0_periodic_H2O.inp`
```
...
 cpuid_vlen           4
 cart. position           1           1 local_data size           0           0
           1           1  0.64524113528718785       0.92805880329027735       0.77797094839274550       ...
 Sparsity of Σ^c(iτ,k=0): Percentage of skipped atom pairs:                0.0 %
 cpuid_vlen           4
 cart. position           0           0 local_data size          23          23
           0           0 -0.86279915637516102      -0.43417956913078526      -0.29319700876391425      ...
 cpuid_vlen           4
 cart. position           0           1 local_data size          23           0
           0           1  0.64524113528718785       0.92805880329027735       0.77797094839274550       ...
 cpuid_vlen           4
 cart. position           1           0 local_data size           0          23
           1           0  0.64524113528718785       0.92805880329027735       0.77797094839274550       ...
...
```

As you can see, ranks at (0,1), (1,0) and (1,1) have at least one size of local_data zero, i.e. they do not contain any data - all data is stored on the rank (0,0). This leads to an error in `cp_cfm_geeig` - the ranks which have empty local data now contain invalid eigenvalues array, which then propagates to other parts of the code.

## Escaping regtesting

Curiously enough, I suspect that this bug would have been caught by the regtesting if the tests ran with at least one other mpiranks combination other than 2 ranks. By a (un)lucky coincidence, the original code used two different numbers in the flooring procedure of the block size (`(fmstruct%context%num_pe(1))*fmstruct%context%num_pe(2)` instead of `(fmstruct%context%num_pe(2))*fmstruct%context%num_pe(2)`) which in the specific 2 rank case leads to correctly setting the blocks such that both contain approximately equal data. Therefore, in the case of 2 mpiranks, this error was not caught by the regtesting.

Indeed, after rerunning the patch print mentioned above in 2x2 setup, the distributions are reasonable and the eigenvalues are identical on both ranks (again , `01_G0W0_periodic_H2O.inp` calculation)
```
...
 Sparsity of Σ^c(iτ,k=0): Percentage of skipped atom pairs:                0.0 %
 cpuid_vlen           4
 cart. position           0           0 local_data size          15          23
           1           0 -0.86279915637515936      -0.43417956913078576      -0.29319700876391325      ...   
 cpuid_vlen           4
 cart. position           1           0 local_data size           8          23
           0           0 -0.86279915637515936      -0.43417956913078576      -0.29319700876391325      ... 
...
```

title: Errors introduced by PR #3859 when using MKL
description:
PR #3859 causes compilation issues when using MKL. Below are the observed errors:

1. **Missing IMPLICIT declaration:**
   ```plaintext
   cp2k/src/fm/cp_fm_basic_linalg.F:314:55:

     314 |                         alpha, aa, nrow_global, beta, b, nrow_global, bb, nrow_global)
         |                                                       1
   Error: Symbol ‘b’ at (1) has no IMPLICIT type; did you mean ‘bb’?
   ```

2. **Unused variables warning:**
   ```plaintext
   cp2k/src/fm/cp_fm_basic_linalg.F:285:19:

     285 |       INTEGER :: ii, jj
         |                   1
   Error: Unused variable ‘ii’ declared at (1) [-Werror=unused-variable]

title: Use of 'TMC_ANALYSIS' with AMD GPUs causes a segfault
description:
When the tests which are in the 'tests/TMC/regtest_ana_on_the_fly/' directory which contain a 'TMC_ANALYSIS' section are run on a system with AMD GPUs (specifically either GFX90A or GFX942), the binary segfaults and creates a core file. If the same input file is used either with Nvidia GPUs or no GPU support (different binaries, of course), the tests work as expected.  Here is an example of what appears in the output:

To build the code, I run the following using a Cray Programming Environment:

1. For the purposes of these instructions, I'll assume the arch file is called 'amd_gfx.psmp', has the contents shared above, and is in the 'arch/' directory.
2. Run: `module load cray-fftw`
3. Also run either `module load craype-accel-amd-gfx90a` or `module load craype-accel-amd-gfx942` depending on the GPU model.
4. Make sure to have 'PrgEnv-gnu' loaded.
5. Either run `module load rocm` or make sure 'ROCM_PATH' is set appropriately.

title: Segfault with test files run against AMD GPUs using 2025.1
description:
Attempting to update our internal copy of CP2K to 'v2025.1', I am finding that our AMD GPU build segfaults when run against with a few of the test files, specifically GFX90A and GFX942 models. The same tests run with the 'v2024.3' version works, so this appears to be a regression. In this specific instance, I am using ROCm 6.1.0.

The test files which are triggering this specific segfault are:
- tests/SE/regtest-2-1/md.inp
- tests/QS/regtest-ot-1/H2O-print.inp
- tests/QS/regtest-gpw-3/NO2-mulliken.inp
- tests/QS/regtest-gpw-4/H2O-debug-2.inp

Here are the contents of the 'arch' file used for AMD GPU builds:
```

# variable to be set as expected.

OFFLOAD_CC     = hipcc
CC             = cc
CXX            = CC
FC             = ftn
LD             = ftn
AR             = ar -r
GPUVER         = Mi250
HIPARCH        = <GPU_MODEL>

DFLAGS         = -D__OFFLOAD_HIP -D__GRID_HIP -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ \
                 -D__FFTW3 -D__parallel -D__SCALAPACK -D__ACC

#
WFLAGS      =
#
FCDEBFLAGS  =
CFLAGS         = -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fopenmp -g -mtune=native  \
                 $(PROFOPT)  -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror \
                 -Wno-vla-parameter -Wno-deprecated-declarations \
		 -Wno-implicit-function-declaration \
                 $(DFLAGS) -I${ROCM_PATH}/include
CXXFLAGS       =  --std=c++14 $(DFLAGS) -Wno-deprecated-declarations \
                  -fopenmp -Wall -Wextra -Werror -I${ROCM_PATH}/include
FCFLAGS        = -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fopenmp -funroll-loops \
                 -ftree-vectorize -ffree-form -ffree-line-length-512 \
                 -g -mtune=native $(PROFOPT)  $(FCDEBFLAGS) $(WFLAGS) \
                 $(DFLAGS) ${allow_arg_mismatch_flag}
LDFLAGS        = $(FCFLAGS) -Wl,--enable-new-dtags -L'${ROCM_PATH}/lib' \
                 -Wl,-rpath,'${ROCM_PATH}/lib'
OFFLOAD_FLAGS  = -g --offload-arch=${HIPARCH} -munsafe-fp-atomics \
                 -fopenmp -m64 -pthread \
                 -fPIC -O3 --std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror \
                 $(DFLAGS) -I${ROCM_PATH}/include
OFFLOAD_TARGET = hip
LIBS           = -lfftw3 -lfftw3_threads -lhipfft
LIBS          += -lstdc++ -lamdhip64
```

To build the code, I run the following using a Cray Programming Environment:
1. For the purposes of these instructions, I'll assume the arch file is called 'amd_gfx.psmp', has the contents shared above, and is in the 'arch/' directory.
2. Run: `module load cray-fftw`
3. Also run either `module load craype-accel-amd-gfx90a` or `module load craype-accel-amd-gfx942` depending on the GPU model.
4. Make sure to have `PrgEnv-gnu` loaded.
5. Either run `module load rocm` or make sure 'ROCM_PATH' is set appropriately.
6. Set: `export allow_arg_mismatch_flag="-fallow-argument-mismatch"`
7. Set: `HIP_MODEL=gfx90a` or `HIP_MODEL=gfx942` depending on the GPU model.
8. Run: `sed -ie -e "s/<GPU_MODEL>/${HIP_MODEL}/" arch/amd_gfx.psmp`
9. Proceed to build it: `CC=cc CXX=CC make -j 4 ARCH=amd_gfx VERSION=psmp`

I will also state that for the 'v2025.1' build, I am using the 'v2.8.0' tagged version of 'dbcsr'; with the 'v2024.3' build, we use the 'v2.7.0' tagged version of 'dbcsr'.

title: Which version of Intel compiler does installation of cp2k 2025.1 (toolchain and itself) support?
description:
Greetings. It seems that a subtle undocumented change made in #3644 has made its way to cp2k 2025.1 compared with 2024.3. In this commit, a new switch between classic Intel Fortran compiler `ifort` and modern Intel OneAPI Fortran compiler `ifx` is added to the toolchain options, but the switch for Intel C/C++ compiler between classical `icc` `icpc` and modern `icx` `icpx` has been dropped and now only the latter is effectively available. Apart from the question in title, here are two more points to clear:
1. Does compiling CP2K with `ifx` really require all dependencies installed with toolchain be built with `icx` `icpx` `ifx`? If two independent toggles between `icc` `icpc`/`icx` `icpx` and `ifort`/`ifx` are available in toolchain options, it could be helpful in some difficult cases.
2. Can the good old [dev:compiler_support](https://www.cp2k.org/dev:compiler_support?rev=1697283524) list be updated to reflect the status? It was last edited in 2023, still saying that Intel compiler is supported for classic 2021.* but not OneAPI 2021.3.
descripcion_limpia: greetings seems subtle undocumented change made made way cpk compared commit new switch classic intel fortran compiler ifort modern intel oneapi fortran compiler ifx added toolchain options switch intel cc compiler classical icc icpc modern icx icpx dropped latter effectively available apart question title two points clear compiling cpk ifx really require dependencies installed toolchain built icx icpx ifx two independent toggles icc icpcicx icpx ifortifx available toolchain options could helpful difficult cases good old devcompilersupport list updated reflect status last edited still saying intel compiler supported classic oneapi

title: Compilation error due to suspected dbcsr update failure
description:
We are wrapping up development of a new electronic structure method in CP2K. To merge our code with the main branch, we followed all steps described at https://www.cp2k.org/dev:starting , including the last step in "Update your copy of the master/rebase your patch" - Step 2c. The merge went fine, but the resulting code fails to compile with the error

"No initializer for component ‘nrows_total’ given in the structure constructor at"

which was previously describe here:

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/issues/3856

As the author of that post, we suspect that dbcsr submodule was not properly updated because (A) git status shows "Changes not staged for commit: modified:   exts/dbcsr (modified content)" and (B) exts/dbcsr/VERSION shows 2.5 version instead of 2.6 or 2.7.

MAJOR = 2
MINOR = 5
PATCH = 0
-# A specific DATE (YYYY-MM-DD) fixes an official release, otherwise
-# it is considered Development version.
DATE  = 2022-12-27

Unfortunately, running "git submodule update --recursive" does not do anything. Are there any other ways to update dbcsr library? 

Is it possible that the issue is not related to dbcsr, but is caused by our compiler, which does not work with the most recent cp2k? We use gcc/12.3 and openmpi/4.1.5. It worked before the recent merge.
descripcion_limpia: wrapping development new electronic structure method cpk merge code main branch followed steps described including last step update copy masterrebase patch step c merge went fine resulting code fails compile error initializer component nrowstotal given structure constructor previously describe author post suspect dbcsr submodule properly updated git status shows changes staged commit modified extsdbcsr modified content b extsdbcsrversion shows version instead major minor patch specific date yyyymmdd fixes official release otherwise considered development version date unfortunately running git submodule update recursive anything ways update dbcsr library possible issue related dbcsr caused compiler work recent cpk use gcc openmpi worked recent merge

Hi, one of our HPC users is excited about CP2K 2025.1 and asked us to install it. I tried to use the current spack recipe and just adding a new version() line. Maybe you have an idea what is going wrong? I don't understand the error.

If you point me in the right direction i can update the spack recipe, i'm familiar with spack, just not with CP2K.

The compilers i tried are GCC 11, 13 and 14. The package compiled fine on our AMD Rome/NVIDIA A100/Infiniband system with GCC 11.4/CUDA 12.2 and GCC 13.2/CUDA 12.6 , this error only appears on the Intel Sapphirerapids/OmniPath system.

title: Bump jinja2 from 3.1.4 to 3.1.5 in /tools/precommit
description:
Bumps [jinja2](https://github.com/pallets/jinja) from 3.1.4 to 3.1.5.
Release notes

Sourced from jinja2's releases.

3.1.5

This is the Jinja 3.1.5 security fix release, which fixes security issues and bugs but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes compared to the latest feature release.

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Jinja2/3.1.5/ Changes: https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/changes/#version-3-1-5 Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/jinja/milestone/16?closed=1

  • The sandboxed environment handles indirect calls to str.format, such as by passing a stored reference to a filter that calls its argument. GHSA-q2x7-8rv6-6q7h
  • Escape template name before formatting it into error messages, to avoid issues with names that contain f-string syntax. #1792, GHSA-gmj6-6f8f-6699
  • Sandbox does not allow clear and pop on known mutable sequence types. #2032
  • Calling sync render for an async template uses asyncio.run. #1952
  • Avoid unclosed auto_aiter warnings. #1960
  • Return an aclose-able AsyncGenerator from Template.generate_async. #1960
  • Avoid leaving root_render_func() unclosed in Template.generate_async. #1960
  • Avoid leaving async generators unclosed in blocks, includes and extends. #1960
  • The runtime uses the correct concat function for the current environment when calling block references. #1701
  • Make |unique async-aware, allowing it to be used after another async-aware filter. #1781
  • |int filter handles OverflowError from scientific notation. #1921
  • Make compiling deterministic for tuple unpacking in a {% set ... %} call. #2021
  • Fix dunder protocol (copy/pickle/etc) interaction with Undefined objects. #2025
  • Fix copy/pickle support for the internal missing object. #2027
  • Environment.overlay(enable_async) is applied correctly. #2061
  • The error message from FileSystemLoader includes the paths that were searched. #1661
  • PackageLoader shows a clearer error message when the package does not contain the templates directory. #1705
  • Improve annotations for methods returning copies. #1880
  • urlize does not add mailto: to values like @a@b. #1870
  • Tests decorated with @pass_context can be used with the |select filter. #1624
  • Using set for multiple assignment (a, b = 1, 2) does not fail when the target is a namespace attribute. #1413
  • Using set in all branches of {% if %}{% elif %}{% else %} blocks does not cause the variable to be considered initially undefined. #1253
Changelog

Sourced from jinja2's changelog.

Version 3.1.5

Released 2024-12-21

  • The sandboxed environment handles indirect calls to str.format, such as by passing a stored reference to a filter that calls its argument. :ghsa:q2x7-8rv6-6q7h
  • Escape template name before formatting it into error messages, to avoid issues with names that contain f-string syntax. :issue:1792, :ghsa:gmj6-6f8f-6699
  • Sandbox does not allow clear and pop on known mutable sequence types. :issue:2032
  • Calling sync render for an async template uses asyncio.run. :pr:1952
  • Avoid unclosed auto_aiter warnings. :pr:1960
  • Return an aclose-able AsyncGenerator from Template.generate_async. :pr:1960
  • Avoid leaving root_render_func() unclosed in Template.generate_async. :pr:1960
  • Avoid leaving async generators unclosed in blocks, includes and extends. :pr:1960
  • The runtime uses the correct concat function for the current environment when calling block references. :issue:1701
  • Make |unique async-aware, allowing it to be used after another async-aware filter. :issue:1781
  • |int filter handles OverflowError from scientific notation. :issue:1921
  • Make compiling deterministic for tuple unpacking in a {% set ... %} call. :issue:2021
  • Fix dunder protocol (copy/pickle/etc) interaction with Undefined objects. :issue:2025
  • Fix copy/pickle support for the internal missing object. :issue:2027
  • Environment.overlay(enable_async) is applied correctly. :pr:2061
  • The error message from FileSystemLoader includes the paths that were searched. :issue:1661
  • PackageLoader shows a clearer error message when the package does not contain the templates directory. :issue:1705
  • Improve annotations for methods returning copies. :pr:1880
  • urlize does not add mailto: to values like @a@b. :pr:1870
  • Tests decorated with @pass_context`` can be used with the ``|select`` filter. :issue:1624`
  • Using set for multiple assignment (a, b = 1, 2) does not fail when the target is a namespace attribute. :issue:1413
  • Using set in all branches of {% if %}{% elif %}{% else %} blocks does not cause the variable to be considered initially undefined. :issue:1253
Commits
  • 877f6e5 release version 3.1.5
  • 8d58859 remove test pypi
  • eda8fe8 update dev dependencies
  • c8fdce1 Fix bug involving calling set on a template parameter within all branches of ...
  • 66587ce Fix bug where set would sometimes fail within if
  • fbc3a69 Add support for namespaces in tuple parsing (#1664)
  • b8f4831 more comments about nsref assignment
  • ee83219 Add support for namespaces in tuple assignment
  • 1d55cdd Triple quotes in docs (#2064)
  • 8a8eafc edit block assignment section

description:
This PR integrates the existing routines for computing exciton descriptors in the BSE module into the TDDFPT module. These descriptors enable benchmarking of excited states beyond energies and oscillator strengths, providing quantitative insights into spatial properties (for details, see, e.g., J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2018, 14, 710–725: https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acs.jctc.7b01145).

I made minimal changes to the TDDFPT module, as this is my first time working with it, and I hope they are fine like that. @annahehn, @juerghutter, @abussy, and others familiar with the TDDFPT module, please feel free to share any suggestions or comments - I’m happy to address them.

Max

title: Berry equivalent to Boys in LOCALIZE module?
description:
Dear CP2K developers and users,
In the CP2K input reference, there are [three operators](https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/FORCE_EVAL/DFT/LOCALIZE.html#CP2K_INPUT.FORCE_EVAL.DFT.LOCALIZE.OPERATOR) for the orbital localization (also called generation of Maximally localized Wannier functions, etc). I tried to read two related papers

[Maximally localized generalized Wannier functions for composite energy bands](https://journals.aps.org/prb/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevB.56.12847)

[Maximally localized Wannier functions: Theory and applications](https://journals.aps.org/rmp/abstract/10.1103/RevModPhys.84.1419)

and found that, it seems for a gamma-point-only problem, the Berry operator is totally equivalent to Boys operator. Is my understanding correct? But why Berry/Boys are documented as two options in the LOCALIZE module?

Thanks!

Occupied ghost states with smearing without Cholesky
description:
For tracking:
If I turn on smearing and turn off the Cholesky-based eigenvalue solver, CP2K introduces eigen states at energy zero. These states are then partially occupied resulting in wrong energies. The reason is that the root of the inverse overlap matrix still works in the AO base/full MO base projecting out the numerically small components leading to these components still being present but with eigenvalue zero. The workaround is to work in a reduced MO base. I already have a fix for it but still have to check all other places on how to deal with this issue (or if it is relevant at all).

title: TDDFPT| Davidson restart bug fix
description: This pull request addresses a bug in the Davidson solver. After a restart, the solver was comparing the newly computed eigenvalues to the previous eigenvalues, which were identical, leading to a false convergence. The eigenvalues are now flushed, ensuring a full-restart of the solver.

title: TREXIO support
description:
This PR enables support for writing [TREXIO](https://github.com/TREX-CoE/trexio) files from a SCF run.
TREXIO is a library that support interoperability between quantum chemistry codes, especially for programs implementing post-SCF methods such as QMC.

The PR adds an external library dependency, which itself depends on HDF5.

Currently, we only support writing trexio files, and not reading them (e.g. to restart a calculation previously run with a different code support trexio).

title: Tests in QS/regtest-as-3 fail with four ompthreads and mpiranks
description:
Running `tests/do_regtest.py` with the command line parameters `--mpiranks 4 --ompthreads 4 
 --maxtasks 32` causes critical errors even though the CP2K installation is correct (I'm quite sure by now at least).

Running it like this with CP2K version 2024.2 produces the following errors:
* in `QS/regtest-as-3/` is produced the critical abort:
```
 *******************************************************************************
 *   ___                                                                       *
 *  /   \                                                                      *
 * [ABORT]                                                                     *
 *  \___/                 sum of local cols not equal global cols              *
 *    |                                                                        *
 *  O/|                                                                        *
 * /| |                                                                        *
 * / \                                                      cp_fm_struct.F:261 *
 *******************************************************************************
```
* and in another test also in `QS/regtest-as-3/` normal failures:
```
    h2_gpw_nostore_group2.inp                                -    TIMED OUT ( 400.28 sec)
    h2_gpw_nostore_group2.inp                                -    TIMED OUT ( 400.26 sec)
    h2_gpw_ht_group2.inp                                     - RUNTIME FAIL (   1.40 sec)
    h2_gpw_ht_group2.inp                                     - RUNTIME FAIL (   1.50 sec)
    h2_gpw_ht_nostore_group2.inp                             - RUNTIME FAIL (   1.47 sec)
    h2_gpw_ht_nostore_group2.inp                             - RUNTIME FAIL (   1.45 sec)
```

CP2K was installed via spack in one case and via the developer's toolchain script in the other case.

In the end I found out via trial and error, that I do need `--maxtasks 32` when running the tests on an HPC cluster in an interactive job. Otherwise, `do_regtest.py` will start too many instances at the same time and then most will time out. 

In the end, `do_regtest.py --maxtasks 32 <path> <version>` ran entirely without errors for the same CP2K binaries (It was `psmp` in all cases).

Yet `--mpiranks 4 --ompthreads 4` seem to be detrimental parameters. I believe this should not happen. Instead the regression test script should skip some tests if they would be in conflict with the `--mpiranks` and `--ompthreads` specifications. 

I hope this report helps to make the installation and regression testing clearer for the users.
descripcion_limpia: running testsdoregtestpy command line parameters mpiranks ompthreads maxtasks causes critical errors even though cpk installation correct im quite sure least running like cpk version produces following errors qsregtestas produced critical abort abort sum local cols equal global cols cpfmstructf another test also qsregtestas normal failures hgpwnostoregroupinp timed sec hgpwnostoregroupinp timed sec hgpwhtgroupinp runtime fail sec hgpwhtgroupinp runtime fail sec hgpwhtnostoregroupinp runtime fail sec hgpwhtnostoregroupinp runtime fail sec cpk installed via spack one case via developers toolchain script case end found via trial error need maxtasks running tests hpc cluster interactive job otherwise doregtestpy start many instances time time end doregtestpy maxtasks ran entirely without errors cpk binaries psmp cases yet mpiranks ompthreads seem detrimental parameters believe happen instead regression test script skip tests would conflict mpiranks ompthreads specifications hope report helps make installation regression testing clearer users

title: Use cusolverMpSygvd - a generalized eigenvalue solver
description:
In `cp_fm_geeig`, instead of reducing the generalized eigenvalue problem into standard from on the CPU and then solving the standard form on the GPU using `cusolverMpSyevd`, solve the whole generalized problem on the GPU using `cusolverMpSygvd`. This gives a nice speedup for all problems which are large enough to benefit from using cuSOLVERMp in the original code. 

The original API for solving only the standard form on the GPU has been kept in the code, should there be a need to use it from some other routine.

title: ELPA: avoid multiple initializations
description:
- Note: potentially ensuring the original "active device" is down to the library (ELPA),
  and CP2K is only responsible for toggling the active device for its own code.
  This note refers to a potential side-effect of (re-)initializing ELPA.
- Avoid potentially high cost of initialization (context creation, etc.)
- Initialization SAVE state does not require atomics (expected to be called from master).
- Harmonized CFM and FM interfaces.
- Code/format cleanup.

title: OMPT: trace OpenMP constructs and check for issues
description:
- Intel OpenMP implemented an OpenMP 5.0 facility called OMPT for tracing OpenMP constructs.
- Intel OpenMP (libiomp5) is open source (libomp), and LLVM compilers provide necessary bits.
- Effectively, Intel Compiler (icx/icc) works out-of-the box (note: ifx/ifort is no prerequisite).
- GNU compiler shall be possible when linking libomp instead of libgomp. Further, currently
  defined(_OPENMP) && defined(__clang__) can be relaxed or smarter/runtime check).
- Currently only one issue is tracked: if a parallel region is opened inside of a master section.
- The OMPT facility has no runtime impact if not enabled, otherwise the impact shall be low.
- The code can be a starting point to enforce more sophisticated conventions.

title: Error in description of velocity
description:
Here https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/input_cp2k_motion_print.F#L110
and (vice versa) here: https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/MOTION/PRINT/VELOCITIES.html,
it is stated that "Having an atom with a mass m in AMU the kinetic energy 1/2mv^2 will be obtained in Hartree (i.e. au) multiplying by 911.447". However, I believe this is incorrect. To me, it seems that C*m[amu]*v^2[atomic units] = E[Ha] for C=911.447, rather than  C*0.5*m[amu]*v^2[atomic units] = E[Ha] as stated in the text. 
My reasoning is as follows:
amu2kg * Bohr2^2 / atomic_units_of_time2s^2 / J2Ha =
= 1,660539040e-27*0,52917721067e-10^2/2.41888E-17^2/4.3597447222071e-18 = 
= 1,8228949765137519199e3
= 2C
descripcion_limpia: vice versa stated atom mass amu kinetic energy mv obtained hartree ie au multiplying however believe incorrect seems cmamuvatomic units eha c rather cmamuvatomic units eha stated text reasoning follows amukg bohr atomicunitsoftimes jha eeee e c

title: Bump werkzeug from 3.0.3 to 3.0.6 in /tools/precommit
description:
Bumps [werkzeug](https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug) from 3.0.3 to 3.0.6.
Release notes

Sourced from werkzeug's releases.

3.0.6

This is the Werkzeug 3.0.6 security fix release, which fixes security issues but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes.

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Werkzeug/3.0.6/ Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/changes/#version-3-0-6

  • Fix how max_form_memory_size is applied when parsing large non-file fields. GHSA-q34m-jh98-gwm2
  • safe_join catches certain paths on Windows that were not caught by ntpath.isabs on Python < 3.11. GHSA-f9vj-2wh5-fj8j

3.0.5

This is the Werkzeug 3.0.5 fix release, which fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes.

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Werkzeug/3.0.5/ Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/changes/#version-3-0-5 Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/milestone/37?closed=1

  • The Watchdog reloader ignores file closed no write events. #2945
  • Logging works with client addresses containing an IPv6 scope. #2952
  • Ignore invalid authorization parameters. #2955
  • Improve type annotation fore SharedDataMiddleware. #2958
  • Compatibility with Python 3.13 when generating debugger pin and the current UID does not have an associated name. #2957

3.0.4

This is the Werkzeug 3.0.4 fix release, which fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes.

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Werkzeug/3.0.4/ Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/3.0.x/changes/#version-3-0-4 Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/milestone/36?closed=1

  • Restore behavior where parsing multipart/x-www-form-urlencoded data with invalid UTF-8 bytes in the body results in no form data parsed rather than a 413 error. #2930
  • Improve parse_options_header performance when parsing unterminated quoted string values. #2904
  • Debugger pin auth is synchronized across threads/processes when tracking failed entries. #2916
  • Dev server handles unexpected SSLEOFError due to issue in Python < 3.13. #2926
  • Debugger pin auth works when the URL already contains a query string. #2918
Changelog

Sourced from werkzeug's changelog.

Version 3.0.6

Released 2024-10-25

  • Fix how max_form_memory_size is applied when parsing large non-file fields. :ghsa:q34m-jh98-gwm2
  • safe_join catches certain paths on Windows that were not caught by ntpath.isabs on Python < 3.11. :ghsa:f9vj-2wh5-fj8j

Version 3.0.5

Released 2024-10-24

  • The Watchdog reloader ignores file closed no write events. :issue:2945
  • Logging works with client addresses containing an IPv6 scope :issue:2952
  • Ignore invalid authorization parameters. :issue:2955
  • Improve type annotation fore SharedDataMiddleware. :issue:2958
  • Compatibility with Python 3.13 when generating debugger pin and the current UID does not have an associated name. :issue:2957

Version 3.0.4

Released 2024-08-21

  • Restore behavior where parsing multipart/x-www-form-urlencoded data with invalid UTF-8 bytes in the body results in no form data parsed rather than a 413 error. :issue:2930
  • Improve parse_options_header performance when parsing unterminated quoted string values. :issue:2904
  • Debugger pin auth is synchronized across threads/processes when tracking failed entries. :issue:2916
  • Dev server handles unexpected SSLEOFError due to issue in Python < 3.13. :issue:2926
  • Debugger pin auth works when the URL already contains a query string. :issue:2918

  • 9caf72a release version 3.0.5

title: Sort various things for build reproducibility
fail to compile when use toolchain with "intel-8488c" cpu
description:
Toolchain scipt is able to auto generate the local.ssmp/pdbg/...  files

If "make" with these files, there will be many errors like  "/tmp/ccqpMdXL.s:3268: Error: no such instruction: `vmovw %xmm5,(%rsi)' ". And the compile will fail.

I find the flags in these files contains "-march=native".  
If I run 
`gcc -march=native -Q --help=target`

and I get :

  -mavx5124fmaps                        [disabled]
  -mavx5124vnniw                        [disabled]
  -mavx512bf16                          [enabled]
  -mavx512bitalg                        [enabled]
  -mavx512bw                            [enabled]
  -mavx512cd                            [enabled]
  -mavx512dq                            [enabled]
  -mavx512er                            [disabled]
  -mavx512f                             [enabled]
  -mavx512fp16                          [enabled]
  -mavx512ifma                          [enabled]
  -mavx512pf                            [disabled]
  -mavx512vbmi                          [enabled]
  -mavx512vbmi2                         [enabled]
  -mavx512vl                            [enabled]
  -mavx512vnni                          [enabled]
  -mavx512vp2intersect                  [disabled]
  -mavx512vpopcntdq                     [enabled]
  -mavxifma                             [disabled]
  -mavxneconvert                        [disabled]
  -mavxvnni                             [enabled]
  -mavxvnniint8                         [disabled]
  -mbionic                              [disabled]


If I run 
`grep flags /proc/cpuinfo`

I get:
flags           : fpu vme de pse tsc msr pae mce cx8 apic sep mtrr pge mca cmov pat pse36 clflush dts acpi mmx fxsr sse sse2 ss ht tm pbe syscall nx pdpe1gb rdtscp lm constant_tsc art arch_perfmon pebs bts rep_good nopl xtopology nonstop_tsc cpuid aperfmperf tsc_known_freq pni pclmulqdq dtes64 monitor ds_cpl vmx smx est tm2 ssse3 sdbg fma cx16 xtpr pdcm pcid dca sse4_1 sse4_2 x2apic movbe popcnt tsc_deadline_timer aes xsave avx f16c rdrand lahf_lm abm 3dnowprefetch cpuid_fault epb cat_l3 cat_l2 cdp_l3 invpcid_single intel_ppin cdp_l2 ssbd mba ibrs ibpb stibp ibrs_enhanced tpr_shadow vnmi flexpriority ept vpid ept_ad fsgsbase tsc_adjust bmi1 avx2 smep bmi2 erms invpcid cqm rdt_a avx512f avx512dq rdseed adx smap avx512ifma clflushopt clwb intel_pt avx512cd sha_ni avx512bw avx512vl xsaveopt xsavec xgetbv1 xsaves cqm_llc cqm_occup_llc cqm_mbm_total cqm_mbm_local split_lock_detect avx_vnni avx512_bf16 wbnoinvd dtherm ida arat pln pts hfi avx512vbmi umip pku ospke waitpkg avx512_vbmi2 gfni vaes vpclmulqdq avx512_vnni avx512_bitalg tme avx512_vpopcntdq la57 rdpid bus_lock_detect cldemote movdiri movdir64b enqcmd fsrm md_clear serialize tsxldtrk pconfig arch_lbr amx_bf16 avx512_fp16 amx_tile amx_int8 flush_l1d arch_capabilities


I notice "avx512bf16" and some other flags are not supported by the cpu flags but still enabled if use "-march=native -mtune=native". Thus, I repalce it with "-march=sapphirerapids -mno-avx512bf16 -mno-avx512bitalg -mno-avx512fp16 -mno-avx512vnni -mno-avx512vpopcntdq -mtune=sapphirerapids" to disable the unsupported flags.
Finally, I was able to compile the cp2k.

I suggest to conduct a test of flags in the toolchain scripts to avoid the error.

[out.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17520706/out.txt)

title: Compilation Error for GPU-Accelerated ELPA using Toolchain
description:
Dear CP2K Developers,

As I mentioned earlier, I am attempting to compile version 24.3 of CP2K. My compilation environment includes the following modules:
Currently Loaded Modulefiles:
  1) compiler/intel/2021.3.0              4) nvidia/cuda/11.3                     7) python/3.8.10
  2) mpi/intelmpi/2021.3.0                5) compiler/cmake/3.23.3
  3) mathlib/fftw/3.3.10_intel21_double   6) compiler/gcc/12.2.0
I encountered the following error:
/public/software/compiler/intel-compiler/2021.3.0/compiler/include/complex:62:3: error: #error "This Intel <complex> is for use only with the Intel C++ compilers!"
   62 | # error "This Intel <complex> is for use only with the Intel C++ compilers!"
      |   ^~~~~
/public/software/compiler/intel-compiler/2021.3.0/compiler/include/complex.h:30:3: error: #error "This Intel <complex.h> is for use with only the Intel compilers!"
   30 | # error "This Intel <complex.h> is for use with only the Intel compilers!"
      |   ^~~~~
ifort: command line warning #10148: option '-fno-lto' not supported
ifort: command line warning #10148: option '-fno-lto' not supported
It seems that the Intel libraries are being loaded when compiling the NVIDIA version of ELPA. Could you advise on how to avoid this issue? If more information is needed, please let me know.

Best regards,
Hao Liu
Let me know if you'd like any further adjustments!

title: ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE param of ATOM module is not consistent between different RUN_TYPEs?
description:
I successfully reproduce the pseudopotential optimization procedure with input provided here:
https://github.com/juerghutter/GTH/blob/master/PBE_NLCC/C/q4/C.inp
However, with the GTH-POTENTIAL yielded by task of `RUN_TYPE PSEUDOPOTENTIAL_OPTIMIZATION`, when I was trying to convert it to UPF format, following the way to write input script like examples provided here:
https://github.com/simonpintarelli/lanthanides-nc-pseudos/blob/master/Ce/Ce.inp
, I find the `ELECTRONIC_CONFIGURATION` param seems to accept value in different manner? The former accpets "[He]", while the latter accepts "CORE":
```
ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE [He] 2s2 2p2
CORE [He]
```
or
```
ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE CORE 2s2 2p2
CORE [He]
```
descripcion_limpia: successfully reproduce pseudopotential optimization procedure input provided however gthpotential yielded task runtype pseudopotentialoptimization trying convert upf format following way write input script like examples provided find electronicconfiguration param seems accept value different manner former accpets latter accepts core electronicstructure p core electronicstructure core p core

title: Refactor startup code
description:
- Initialization was updated to cover a consistent set in every place where components are initialized
- Order of initialization/finalization was updated to be consistent in every place
- Farming is not embraced anymore by finalization and initialization
- Main reason for weird farming setup was to set io_unit in DBCSR
- DBCSR supports updating io_unit without tear-down/init cycle
- Initialization/finalization is aimed to be sole business of F77 interface
- F77 interface is also used to setup CP2K PROGRAM hence no need to do init/fini in cp2k_run
- F77 interface updated to more complete set of components, e.g., covering FPGA, DBM, ...
- Sirius is now initialized/finalized unconditionally (less convoluted)
descripcion_limpia: initialization updated cover consistent set every place components initialized order initializationfinalization updated consistent every place farming embraced anymore finalization initialization main reason weird farming setup set iounit dbcsr dbcsr supports updating iounit without teardowninit cycle initializationfinalization aimed sole business f interface f interface also used setup cpk program hence need initfini cpkrun f interface updated complete set components eg covering fpga dbm sirius initializedfinalized unconditionally less convoluted

title: FFT_TOOLS: thread-safety insurance (CPASSERT) and OMP ATOMIC
description:
The motivation for this PR stems from occasional error messages like:

> Fortran runtime error: Pointer actual argument 'fft_scratch_new' is not associated

In any case, the fft_tools manipulated global/SAVE variables without protection for data races.
descripcion_limpia: motivation pr stems occasional error messages like fortran runtime error pointer actual argument fftscratchnew associated case ffttools manipulated globalsave variables without protection data races

title: Automatic basis set creation not compatible with complicated basis sets
description: If RI-basis sets are optimized (using `AUTO_BASIS RI_AUX`) and the primary basis set contains of several basis sets for a given kind (for instance DZ basis set and a seperate set of diffuse functions), then the second basis set is omitted. The workaround is to merge both basis sets into a single one.
descripcion_limpia: ribasis sets optimized using autobasis riaux primary basis set contains several basis sets given kind instance dz basis set seperate set diffuse functions second basis set omitted workaround merge basis sets single one

title: CMake should not check for libxcf90 since only libxcf03 is used.
description:
Although CP2K is correctly only using the interface provided by libxcf03, for some reason the CMake code tries to find libxcf90 and wants to link it in as well. This makes no sense, since libxcf90 and libxcf03 are different beasts, moreover, libxcf90 has been dropped in libxc 7.0.0.

This patch is necessary to build cp2k against up-to-date releases of Libxc.
descripcion_limpia: although cpk correctly using interface provided libxcf reason cmake code tries find libxcf wants link well makes sense since libxcf libxcf different beasts moreover libxcf dropped libxc patch necessary build cpk uptodate releases libxc

title: CPABORT if spline interpolators are used
description:
The transition between realspace and PW grids is by default done with FFTs (compare https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/FORCE_EVAL/DFT/MGRID/INTERPOLATOR.html#CP2K_INPUT.FORCE_EVAL.DFT.MGRID.INTERPOLATOR.KIND). If I switch to the SPLINE3 interpolator, CP2K aborts at line 1037 in pw/pw_spline_utils.F (issues with bounds) whereas the interpolator SPLINE3_NOPBC aborts at line 214 in pw_env/rs_pw_interface.F (not implemented).
The regtests do not cover any of these two cases. I am currently tracking down the SPLINE3 case and the bug is present at least since version 9.1.
descripcion_limpia: transition realspace pw grids default done ffts compare switch spline interpolator cpk aborts line pwpwsplineutilsf issues bounds whereas interpolator splinenopbc aborts line pwenvrspwinterfacef implemented regtests cover two cases currently tracking spline case bug present least since version

title: CP2K+SMEAGOL interface for electron transport with non-equilibrium Green’s functions
description:
A new interface between CP2K and the non-equilibrium Green’s functions (NEGF) code SMEAGOL, as recently published in the Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation.

The SMEAGOL code can be downloaded by contacting Dr. Clotilde Cucinotta at cucinotta@imperial.ac.uk or Prof. Stefano Sanvito at sanvitos@tcd.ie.

Ahart, C. S., Chulkov, S. K. & Cucinotta, C. S. Enabling Ab-Initio Molecular Dynamics under Bias: The CP2K+SMEAGOL Interface for Integrating Density Functional Theory and Non-Equilibrium Green Functions. J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2024.

title: MO Eigenvalues/Kpoints
description:
When doing a DFT calculation with kpoint mesh the Eigenvalues of the MOs (and thus the band gap) are not properly written to the Eigenvalue file (cp2k 2024.1). All kpoints are listed in the cp2k output file as being part of the mesh but then they do not appear in the Eigenvalue files (Symmetry is turned of so that should not be the reason - also the Gamma point is usually affected). I am not sure if this problem then also translates to the density of states. 

  &MO
   EIGENVALUES
    FILENAME eigenvalues_kpoints
    ADD_LAST NUMERIC
 &END MO

title: Real time Bethe-Salpeter Propagation
description:
We introduce the possibility of running the [real time Bethe-Salpeter](http://dx.doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevB.84.245110) propagation for the determination of the polarizability/absorption spectra of molecules.

This code enables an independent method for deriving formally identical results to the Casida equation based BSE approach, with possibilities for extension to stronger fields/time-dependent phenomena.

We have created elementary documentation, ran the regression testing and added new regtests, which were also checked for leaks using the valgrind tool. Precommit checks were run as well.

We plan to further develop and evaluate the performance of this method, especially compared to the Casida equation BSE approach, as we hope that our method will have a better memory scaling for larger systems. Nevertheless, we believe the state of the code right now is sufficient for an introduction to the CP2K community.

title: IGNORE_CONVERGENCE_FAILURE for SIRIUS SCF
description:
This is rather a suggestion than an issue.
A parameter similar to IGNORE_CONVERGENCE_FAILURE in the DFT/SCF section is necessary for SIRIUS calculations. 
Currently, if SIRIUS SCF is converge after N iterations, the following message is printed:
[find] converged after N SCF iterations!
Otherwise, nothing is printed and the calculations (e.g. cell optimization) continue. 
I think a warning message should be printed if SCF is not converged and a parameter similar to  IGNORE_CONVERGENCE_FAILURE is also needed. 
Thanks!

title: Add Z-matrix formalism for linear response
description:
Hi there,

We would like to submit a pull request for the implementation of Z-matrix formalism as presented in [this paper](https://pubs.aip.org/aip/jcp/article/81/11/5031/87334/On-the-evaluation-of-analytic-energy-derivatives), which is a method that can speed up the DFPT (linear response) calculation. Z-matrix can reduce the computational complexity of the linear response calculations from 3N to 3, where N is the number of atoms in a system.

The users can turn on the Z-matrix method using the keyword `Z_MATRIX_METHOD` under the `DCDR`'s linear response deck. The regtest files for this implementation can also be found in `cp2k/tests/QS/regtest-dcdr/` folder.

Regarding the test, we have performed the compilations and full regtest using `sdbg`, `pdbg`, and `psmp` with no errors or memory leaks detected. Prettifying has also been done.

We believe that this contribution would benefit the CP2K's users.

Best regards,
Ravi (@ravikumar381709) and Rangsiman

title: Accurate forces for hybrid functionals / exact exchange
description:
Hi, 
For most GGA functionals, I succeed in reducing numerical noise on the forces down to the order of meV/angstrom by increasing NGRIDS, REL_CUTOFF, CUTOFF, and decreasing the EPS_SCF values. However, for calculations with exact exchange, I can't get this to work. For a unit cell with ~400 atoms (H, C, O, Zr), I'm still obtaining a total net force of about 200 meV/angstrom, although this value should be close to zero.

I've pasted my input settings below. Is there anything I can still change or tighten in order to reduce the numerical noise?

```
&FORCE_EVAL
  &PRINT
	&FORCES
	&END FORCES
  &END PRINT
  &DFT
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_MOLOPT_UZH
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_ADMM_UZH
    POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME POTENTIAL_UZH
    &MGRID
      CUTOFF 800
      REL_CUTOFF 60
      NGRIDS 6
    &END MGRID
    &SCF
      MAX_SCF 20
      EPS_SCF 1.0E-8
      &OT
        MINIMIZER CG
        PRECONDITIONER FULL_ALL
      &END OT
      &OUTER_SCF
        MAX_SCF 20
        EPS_SCF 1.0E-8
      &END OUTER_SCF
    &END SCF
    &QS
      EPS_DEFAULT 1.0E-12
      EPS_PGF_ORB 1.0E-16
      EPS_FILTER_MATRIX 0.0e0

    &END QS
    &XC # revPBE0-TC-D3

title: How to gracefully handle OOM
description:
Is it possible to deal with OOM situations gracefully? 

I'm dealing with input structures which are somewhat unphysical in terms of box size and or particle positions. It's hard to filter this out beforehand. In any case, when he tries to construct the PW basis, he runs OOM because probably the number of plane waves is exceptionally high because the box is unnecessarily large.

The problem is that it triggers an OOM of the kind that it kills everything else that was running on the node. Ideally, I'd use e.g `ulimit` to handle this more gracefully. When I use `ulimit -v ` and set it to 2GB (roughly the available memory per core, those errors are in fact successfully caught by the OS and it no longer makes everything crash. However, sometimes, it also triggers OOM errors in cases where he's clearly not running out of memory. Presumably, the ulimit kicks in a little to early, i.e. before spawning all the separate processes, since in those cases there hasn't been any CP2K output whatsoever.

description:
Hello **cp2k** community,

I'm planning to run some Ab Initio Molecular Dynamics (AIMD) simulations on perfect crystal systems. I have a question regarding the use of supercells: **Is it always necessary to use an appropriately sized supercell for AIMD simulations, even when dealing with perfect crystals?**

I understand that supercells are crucial for studying defects and can help with issues like phonon sampling and finite size effects. However, I'm unsure if these considerations are as important for a perfect crystal structure. Specifically, I'd appreciate insights on:

1. The main advantages of using a supercell for perfect crystals in AIMD.
2. Potential drawbacks or inaccuracies that might arise from using a smaller cell.
3. Guidelines for determining an appropriate supercell size for AIMD of perfect crystals.
4. Any computational tricks or settings in **cp2k** that could help balance accuracy and efficiency when choosing supercell size.

Thank you in advance for your expertise and advice!

Regards,
Zhao

title: mpiwrap: get number of processes per system
description:
A service function to determine the number of ranks per node can help to make CP2K easier to use.

For example, there are various place requiring something like `MAX_MEMORY` per MPI-process. An input file/section may be easier to port over to a different environment/system if the maximum amount of memory per process can be not only given in any sensible unit (Megabyte, Gigabyte, etc) but rather determined automatically like `MAX_MEMORY=-1` or `MAX_MEMORY=auto`.

Another case can be advice on feasible rank counts under certain algorithmic constrains/optimality like total rank-count to be a square number, i.e., ranks-per-system can be hinted (console output). Also, cases like full population of MPI-ranks per system can be detected and hints can be issued accordingly (to improve scalability or memory consumption).

title: Printing MOs in Cartesian basis forces calculation of all virtuals
description:
Enabling the option `PRINT%MO%CARTESIAN` automatically forces the calculation of all virtual orbitals. This is set here: https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/4aa16b4ca3f76a073859a3b975508881fe8694f3/src/qs_environment.F#L1580-L1587

However, as one can see from here,
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/4aa16b4ca3f76a073859a3b975508881fe8694f3/src/qs_mo_io.F#L1221-L1224
it is not really required, since having `nmo` set to the number of occupied (or requested) MOs rather than the total number of AOs will not affect the transformation, and will not require the calculation of the extra (potentially unwanted) MOs.
descripcion_limpia: enabling option printmocartesian automatically forces calculation virtual orbitals set however one see really required since nmo set number occupied requested mos rather total number aos affect transformation require calculation extra potentially unwanted mos

title: Question about electronic entropy and Fermi-Dirac smearing
description:
Dear CP2K developers and users,

When the Fermi-Dirac smearing is invoked in CP2K, are analytical gradients the derivatives of E or F? A screenshot of the VASP wiki is shown as follows

![VASP_wiki_smear](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23a4878b-b8db-490a-a780-6de6c5ee9555)


The CP2K output for the SCF energy is like the following
```
...
  *** SCF run converged in   260 steps ***
...
  Overlap energy of the core charge distribution:               0.00000588578631
  Self energy of the core charge distribution:             -16583.49736545768974
  Core Hamiltonian energy:                                   5606.86774144273295
  Hartree energy:                                             616.37096636768126
  Exchange-correlation energy:                              -1995.52233159768321
  Dispersion energy:                                           -4.13358175936631
  Electronic entropic energy:                                  -0.02148917704246
  Fermi energy:                                                -0.04520749773420

  Total energy:                                            -12359.93605429535273
```

I check and find that the sum of all sub-terms above (except the Fermi energy) is equal to the `Total energy`. It seems the electronic entropy is included in the total electronic energy. But are the derivatives of electronic entropy also included in the following analytical gradient calculation? Thanks for any help.

Best,
Jingxiang

title: FFTW fails with non-default FFTW_PLAN_TYPE
description:
#2686 apparently introduced a bug such that calculations fail if FFTW_PLAN_TYPE has a different value than ESTIMATE (default) and FFTW3 tries to import a wisdom file. Currently, there are at least two bugs, one is an incorrect conversion of Fortran characters to C strings (easy to fix) but still, CP2K fails if FFTW3 attempts to import a wisdom file. Exporting wisdom does work after fixing the conversion issue locally.

The current workaround is to use ESTIMATE mode and not reading from a wisdom file. This is usual not a performance issue for 3D periodic systems because they are usually dominated by grid operations and/or DBCSR.

Currently, I cannot reproduce the issue with a simple CP2K-independent tester.

title: dbt: reshape contended by memory allocations
description:
Procedure [dbt_reshape](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/dbt/dbt_reshape_ops.F#L55) can be contended by memory allocations. Fortran's ALLOCATE is called on a per-thread basis with some ALLOCATEs in a per-thread-loop. Further, `dbt_reshape` may be called somewhat frequently (repeating the allocation pattern). Contention has not been fully root-caused (Linux distribution, GLIBC version, compiler), but spread ranges from insignificant to major impact.

If code structure remains as is, the least impacting change seems to rely on the [OpenMP ALLOCATE directive](https://www.openmp.org/spec-html/5.0/openmpsu54.html). This directive was introduced in OpenMP 5.0 (some arguments like `align` came later). Hence, it is suggested to protect the directive like:

> #if defined(_OPENMP) && (201811 <= _OPENMP)
      !$OMP ALLOCATE(myvars-comma-separated-list)
#endif

GNU Fortran introduced support only with GCC 14 *and* `-fopenmp-allocators` is required (see [here](https://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gfortran/Fortran-Dialect-Options.html#index-fopenmp-allocators)). The flag actually suggests an own preprocessor symbol to avoid warnings if `201811 <= _OPENMP` but no flag was given:

> #if defined(_OPENMP) && (201811 <= _OPENMP)
#define CP_OMP_ALLOCS
#endif
...
...
#if defined(CP_OMP_ALLOCS)
      !$OMP ALLOCATE(myvars-comma-separated-list)
#endif

An alternative approach (perhaps preferred) is to introduce a pool for `block_buffer_type`. Using the Fortran API of OpenMP (`omp_malloc`) or MPI based memory allocation (`mpi_alloc_mem`) is then feasible since encapsulated.

title: Conventions: Fix uninitialised variables and missing default initializers
description:
This PR:
- fixes uninitialised variables
- adds default initializers for most types
- adds an implicit interface to one module
- removes ~300 suppressions of the convention checker

Default initializers are still missing due if the respective type member variables of the libraries
- DBCSR
- SIRIUS (PR in progress: electronic-structure/SIRIUS#1005)
- PEXSI

For the record:
- Types derived from the abstract type eri_type_eri_element_func may be false positives as all their member variables have a default (the base type does not have any member variables)
- semi_empirical_int_debug could be reimplemented using submodules (requiring adjustments on the build system) due to dependencies between this file and semi_empirical_int_utils

title: Fix AXK implementation and extend it to SOSEX
description:
This PR
- fixes the legacy implementation of RPA-AXK
- adds the SOSEX kernel
- implements a second implementation employing the original integrals guaranteeing cancellation of terms in condensed phase with Coulomb correlation potential and truncated Coulomb HFX potential. This implementation has a worse scaling than the HFX-based implementation
- misc. refactorings to cp_fm_structs (bind index conversion functions to the type)

Changes to the input:
- the AXK keyword is replaced by an EXCHANGE_CORRECTION section
- The keyword USE_HFX_IMPLEMENTATION switches to the legacy-like HFX-based implementation
- BLOCK_SIZE increases the internal block size of the new implementation employing DGEMM/SpLA. The larger the blocksize the more memory is required but the better the performance.

Annotation:
- I observe numerical issues introducing potential errors at the order of 0.1 mEh for both implementations (the new implementation has lower errors).
- The new implementation could be replaced with a RI-MP2-like implementation which requires more refactoring on that side (future work) which could have better numerical properties.

title: Ambiguous Tag in the output file when ran cell optimizations
description:
Dear CP2K developers,

I found an ambiguous tag in the output file (`.out`) when I ran cell optimizations. The details are presented as follows.

In the "Convergence check" section, when neither 'Max. gradient' nor 'RMS gradient' meets the convergence criteria, their tags are both displayed as "**Conv. for gradients**        =                   No".  For ease of reference, I have marked the position of the tags with arrows.

```
 --------  Informations at step =    62 ------------
  Optimization Method        =                 BFGS
  Total Energy               =      -699.7050944533
  Internal Pressure [bar]    =      -429.0176709974
  Real energy change         =        -0.0001072320
  Predicted change in energy =        -0.0000762510
  Scaling factor             =         0.4741653427
  Step size                  =         0.0110589295
  Trust radius               =         0.3779452266
  Decrease in energy         =                  YES
  Used time                  =               37.476

  Convergence check :
  Max. step size             =         0.0110589295
  Conv. limit for step size  =         0.0030000000
  Convergence in step size   =                   NO
  RMS step size              =         0.0029402551
  Conv. limit for RMS step   =         0.0015000000
  Convergence in RMS step    =                   NO
  Max. gradient              =         0.0009201371
  Conv. limit for gradients  =         0.0004500000
  Conv. for gradients        =                   NO    <--------      
  RMS gradient               =         0.0003096221
  Conv. limit for RMS grad.  =         0.0003000000                                                                                                                        
  Conv. for gradients        =                   NO    <========
  Pressure Deviation [bar]   =      -430.0309209974
  Pressure Tolerance [bar]   =       100.0000000000
  Conv. for  PRESSURE        =                   NO
 ---------------------------------------------------
```
The same tags of **Conv. for gradients** can lead to confusion.


Once the 'RMS gradient' meets the convergence criteria, however, it displays as "**Conv. in RMS gradients**". Please see below. 

```
 --------  Informations at step =    63 ------------
  Optimization Method        =                 BFGS
  Total Energy               =      -699.7051809655
  Internal Pressure [bar]    =      -517.1321714658
  Real energy change         =        -0.0000865121
  Predicted change in energy =        -0.0000611498
  Scaling factor             =         0.4741653427
  Step size                  =         0.0082591465
  Trust radius               =         0.3779452266
  Decrease in energy         =                  YES
  Used time                  =               37.187

  Convergence check :
  Max. step size             =         0.0082591465
  Conv. limit for step size  =         0.0030000000
  Convergence in step size   =                   NO
  RMS step size              =         0.0022719608
  Conv. limit for RMS step   =         0.0015000000
  Convergence in RMS step    =                   NO
  Max. gradient              =         0.0010533890
  Conv. limit for gradients  =         0.0004500000
  Conv. for gradients        =                   NO    <--------
  RMS gradient               =         0.0002811560
  Conv. limit for RMS grad.  =         0.0003000000                                                                                                                        
  Conv. in RMS gradients     =                  YES    <========
  Pressure Deviation [bar]   =      -518.1454214658
  Pressure Tolerance [bar]   =       100.0000000000
  Conv. for  PRESSURE        =                   NO
 ---------------------------------------------------
```
**As shown above, the CP2K program already includes the tag "Conv. in RMS gradients"**, which indicates that the **RMS gradients** have converged, contrasting the earlier ambiguous label "Conv. for gradients". 

It is recommended that developers address the mentioned bug so that when 'RMS gradient' does not meet the convergence criteria, it displays as "**Conv. in RMS gradients**   =   No".

Thanks a lot.
Huan

i–PI server functionality
description:
# i–PI force evaluation method & Nix building
This PR adds two features:
- A new method in `FORCE_EVAL` called `IPI`.
- A set of files that allow CP2K to be built using the [Nix package manager](https://nixos.org/), contributed by @sheepforce

The motivation for these changes is to open up the power of CP2K as a MD/Optimization engine to other software, so that other programs may take advantage of the advanced features of CP2K using their own force evaluation methods. The original goal was to interface our multiscale and fragment calculation package [Spicy](https://gitlab.com/theoretical-chemistry-jena/quantum-chemistry/Spicy) with CP2K. However, since communication takes place through the communications protocol established by [i–PI](https://ipi-code.org/), any code that can communicate this way could be interfaced to CP2K with out further modifications (or so we hope).

## i–PI protocol & implementation
i–PI itself is an engine built for path integral molecular dynamics build on a "server–client" division of labour. i–PI takes the role of the server and handles the evolution of the system, while one or more clients are responsible for the evaluation of the atomic forces. The server communicates with the clients through [network sockets](https://www.gnu.org/software/libc/manual/html_node/Sockets.html), sending atomic positions and cell data and receiving forces in turn. Here, the communications protocol (documented mostly by implementation…) established by i–PI is reused to provide as universal an interface as possible.

CP2K has previously implemented i–PI client side functionality (as a runtype), meaning that it has been able to compute forces for an i–PI server. Here, the other side of the coin is implemented, where an external program provides forces for CP2K. Existing infrastructure is reused where possible.

Example input:
```
&GLOBAL
  RUN_TYPE MD
&END GLOBAL

&FORCE_EVAL
  METHOD IPI
  &SUBSYS
    &CELL
      ABC 10.0 10.0 10.0
      PERIODIC NONE
    &END CELL
    &TOPOLOGY
      COORD_FILE_FORMAT xyz
      COORD_FILE_NAME yourMoleculeHere.xyz
    &END TOPOLOGY
  &END SUBSYS
&END FORCE_EVAL

&MOTION
  &MD
    STEPS 1000
    TIMESTEP 0.25
    ENSEMBLE NVT
    TEMPERATURE 300
    &THERMOSTAT
      TYPE CSVR
      REGION MASSIVE
      &CSVR
        TIMECON 25
      &END CSVR
    &END THERMOSTAT
  &END MD
  &DRIVER
    HOST myHost
    PORT 8421
    UNIX
    SLEEP_TIME 0.1
  &END DRIVER
&END MOTION
```
Note the `DRIVER` subsection, which gives the precise socket location and type.

## Nix
Nix is a package manager and build tool focused on transferability and reproducibility. The files added (`flake.*`, `nix/overlay.nix`, `nix/cp2k.nix`) provide a reproducible build and development environment for CP2K without interfering with any existing infrastructure.

Usage is simple:
- Install Nix
- `nix build` will build/download all dependencies and build CP2K
- `nix develop` will enter a development environment with all dependencies, `fortls`, and `fprettify`

## Possible issues
- This is my first time working on production–level Fortran code, so please review carefully.
- I'm not quite sure how what has been implemented interacts with CP2Ks parallelism. In practice, I don't think parallelism is required for an i–PI server, but it would be good if behaviour was correct anyway.
- This implementation is limited to a single client.

title: FREE_HFX_BUFFER should be FALSE by default in MP2
description:
For MP2 gradients, I think that the default value of FREE_HFX_BUFFER should be set to FALSE rather than TRUE.

With the current default, calculations almost systematically crash. Moreover, new users of MP2 typically have no idea of what causes the crash, and spend valuable time debugging the input file. This is a waste, especially since this is common knowledge among developers. 

What do you think @fstein93 ?

title: CMake can't find spglib on Ubuntu/Debian
description:
When building with `-DCP2K_USE_SPGLIB=ON` on Ubuntu 2024.04, I get the following error message:
```
CMake Error at CMakeLists.txt:686 (find_package):
  Could not find a package configuration file provided by "Spglib" with any
  of the following names:

    SpglibConfig.cmake
    spglib-config.cmake

  Add the installation prefix of "Spglib" to CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH or set
  "Spglib_DIR" to a directory containing one of the above files.  If "Spglib"
  provides a separate development package or SDK, be sure it has been
  installed.
```

It turns out that the [libspglib-f08-dev](https://packages.ubuntu.com/hu/noble/amd64/libspglib-f08-dev/filelist) package ships cmake files with somewhat different names:

```
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/Spglib/SpglibTargets_fortran-none.cmake
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/Spglib/SpglibTargets_fortran.cmake
```

title: Strided writes to abcint in abc_contract_xsmm
description: Currently, "abcint" (INOUT) of `abc_contract_xsmm` is a temporary (work-)array, which is then applied to the desired/actual destination using two strides (three-dimensional array). The goal of this PR is to write the desired destination right-away at the expense of using multiple DGEMV rather than a reasonable-sized DGEMM. The bet is memory ops are overwhelming let alone the intermediate work-array. If this PR proofs beneficial, it enables further benefits like combining `abc_contract_xsmm` and `abc_contract` with the former still requiring pre-transposed `sphi_a`. On top, other inputs to abc_contract may be of temporary/prepared nature rather than using original data; this may also go away if this PR succeeds.
