title: Issue with Pulay mixing and AMD
description:
Hello,

I did compile CP2K 9.1 with the intel compiler 2021 (see below) and I'm trying to run the [example optimization](https://www.cp2k.org/howto:geometry_optimisation). 

```
$ ifort --version
ifort (IFORT) 2021.4.0 20210910
Copyright (C) 1985-2021 Intel Corporation.  All rights reserved.
```


However, after some optimization steps (and many converged SCF), the program failed with a strange error:

```
SCF WAVEFUNCTION OPTIMIZATION

  Step     Update method      Time    Convergence         Total energy    Change
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
     1 Pulay/Diag. 0.50E+00    0.2     0.09642941       -17.1629936995 -1.72E+01
     2 Pulay/Diag. 0.50E+00    0.8     6.65045405     26095.0471698560  2.61E+04

 *******************************************************************************
 *   ___                                                                       *
 *  /   \                                                                      *
 * [ABORT]                                                                     *
 *  \___/    Bad condition number R_COND = 3.357E-18 (smaller than the machine *
 *    |                             working precision)                         *
 *  O/|                                                                        *
 * /| |                                                                        *
 * / \                                                    common/mathlib.F:744 *
 *******************************************************************************


 ===== Routine Calling Stack ===== 

           15 pulay_mixing
           14 gspace_mixing
           13 scf_env_do_scf_inner_loop
           12 scf_env_do_scf
           11 qs_energies
           10 qs_forces
            9 cp_eval_at
            8 cg_deval1d
            7 cg_dbrent
            6 linmin_gold
            5 cg_linmin
            4 cp_cg_main
            3 geoopt_cg
            2 cp_geo_opt
            1 CP2K
```

Complete log available here: [H2O.log](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/9811308/H2O.log). It seems that for some reason, the SCF drift away. And there is more: the energy for this last SCF step (the one before the error) is not the same if I run the program twice. Finally, if I use 4 MPI process, the same error happens, but in the first optimization step (see [H2O_mpirun.log](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/9811323/H2O_mpirun.log)).

This behavior is quite strange, and an issue with intel MPI is also possible. Would you recommend a test with GCC, or is there something else at work here?

title: Unable to build successfully with HIP backends
description:
When I try to include the flags `-D__OFFLOAD_HIP`, my compilation fails. The current build system is very confusing and the following are big concerns in my mind:
- Now we are including all HIP or CUDA backends by default and are having to exclude different backends if we choose to instead of following the general practice of choosing to include only the backends we want to have. This results in files not needed getting included in builds (see examples below).
- When I build for AMD GPUs, the build system seems to include the CUDA backend also? This could be a lack of understanding on my part, but this is causing problems.

Here is an example of building with `-D__NO_OFFLOAD_PW -D__OFFLOAD_HIP`. Note that `src/dbm/*.cu` and `src/grid/gpu/*.cu` files are being built by hipcc. I also see the `src/grid/hip/*` files being built with hipcc, but there is no problem there. I am able to exclude the dbm backend using `__NO_OFFLOAD_DBM` but the problem with the `grid/gpu` files remains.
```
[2022-10-12T00:08:26.459Z] hipcc -c -D__FFTW3 -D__parallel -D__COSMA -D__SCALAPACK -D__LIBXSMM -D__NO_OFFLOAD_PW -D__OFFLOAD_HIP -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -I/opt/fftw/include  -D__LIBINT -D__MAX_CONTR=4 -D__LIBXC --amdgpu-target=gfx90a,gfx908,gfx906 -O3 -fdenormal-fp-math=ieee -fcuda-flush-denormals-to-zero -munsafe-fp-atomics -fPIC --std=c++11 /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.cu
[2022-10-12T00:08:26.962Z] hipcc -c -D__FFTW3 -D__parallel -D__COSMA -D__SCALAPACK -D__LIBXSMM -D__NO_OFFLOAD_PW -D__OFFLOAD_HIP -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -I/opt/fftw/include  -D__LIBINT -D__MAX_CONTR=4 -D__LIBXC --amdgpu-target=gfx90a,gfx908,gfx906 -O3 -fdenormal-fp-math=ieee -fcuda-flush-denormals-to-zero -munsafe-fp-atomics -fPIC --std=c++11 /opt/cp2k/src/grid/gpu/grid_gpu_task_list.cu
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] [91mIn file included from /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.cu:15:
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] In file included from /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.h:15:
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] In file included from /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_internal.h:12:
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_shard.h:10:10: fatal error: 'omp.h' file not found
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] #include <omp.h>
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z]          ^~~~~~~
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] [0m[91m1 error generated when compiling for gfx906.
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] [0m[91mmake[3]: *** [/opt/cp2k/Makefile:521: dbm_multiply_gpu.o] Error 1
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] make[3]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z] [0m[91m/opt/cp2k/src/grid/gpu/grid_gpu_task_list.cu:12:10: fatal error: 'omp.h' file not found
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z] #include <omp.h>
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z]          ^~~~~~~
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z] [0m[91m1 error generated when compiling for gfx906.
```
These `*.cu` files have some openmp pragmas for which we need to include the `-fopenmp` flag when compiling them. There is a problem with including `-fopenmp` in files compiled with `hipcc` because `hipcc` pulls in the openmp library found in llvm during linking. And since I use GNU compilers for everything else, we end up with `libomp.so` and `libgomp.so` in the final linked binary and performance drops.

If we can remove those openmp pragmas in these files or separate device code and host code into different translation units (compiled with different compilers), that would be one solution. I'd rather not build these files if they are not needed anyway.

hang with Intel LLVM-based compiler
description:
Hello,

I am on the Intel Fortran compiler team and work on ifx. (I was also a heavy user of CP2K during my physics post-doc back in the day.)

We would really like CP2K to be able to run correctly with ifx. At the moment, it is hanging soon after start-up with the H2O benchmarks. I have been able to trace the issue back to the timers in the timeset routine in `src/common/timings.F`:
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L228-L229
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L280-L281
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L308-L309
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L383-L384

You have in two places this timeset routines that look like this:
```
!$OMP MASTER
!$OMP CRITICAL(time_setstop)
...
!$OMP END CRITICAL(time_setstop)
!$OMP END MASTER
```

The CRITICAL inside the MASTER is redundant and unfortunately causing issues with Intel OpenMP (based on the LLVM OpenMP). MASTER means only the MASTER thread will execute, so there is no need to have a nested CRITICAL.

The simple solution is to get rid of the !$OMP CRITICAL and !$OMP END CRITICAL sections statements in the lines that I have indicated. Is the CP2K team willing and able to do this?

Thanks in advance,
Nick Romero
descripcion_limpia: hello intel fortran compiler team work ifx also heavy user cpk physics postdoc back day would really like cpk able run correctly ifx moment hanging soon startup ho benchmarks able trace issue back timers timeset routine srccommontimingsf two places timeset routines look like omp master omp criticaltimesetstop omp end criticaltimesetstop omp end master critical inside master redundant unfortunately causing issues intel openmp based llvm openmp master means master thread execute need nested critical simple solution get rid omp critical omp end critical sections statements lines indicated cpk team willing able thanks advance nick romero

Add RPA gradients
description:
This PR adds gradients of the quartically scaling RPA kernel. The code was tested locally and on Piz Daint but not yet on LUMI-G. The code was optimized to reduce the memory footprint on CP2K side. It reuses as much of the SOS-MP2 and MP2-gradient code as possible.

The code employs COSMA but might require significantly more memory than before. Local DGEMM calls make use of SpLA. 

To my experience, the memory requirements increase significantly with COSMA (64 water molecules run well, larger systems don't because of COSMA). I do not know how SpLA increases the requirements. Thus, some more tuning might be required. Probably, machines with more memory like LUMI-G might not be affected.

This PR replaces the SIZE_INTEG_FREQ_GROUP keywords by the NUM_INTEG_GROUP keywords because of easier use.

title: RI-RPA| Allow for XC correction in ADMM RI-RPA
description:
This PR is a general upgrade of RPA when ADMM is used. There are 3 main aspects:

1) Previously, any usage of ADMM when doing RPA calculations (switched on with `RI_RPA%ADMM`) ignored the GGA exchange correction. This simply meant doing HFX in a smaller basis, which is a rather strong approximation. This is now fixed, also for GW.

2) Because ADMM operations in RPA rely on the `qs_env%admm_env`, it was not possible to do ADMM RPA@PBE, which is arguably the most efficient way of doing RPA. This is now possible (with XC correction) thanks to a small refactoring of general ADMM. This also means that RPA specific `xc_section_aux` and `xc_section_primary` sections are created and stored.

3) The  `RI_RPA%HF` input section governing EXX calculations is, in most cases, identical to the `DFT%XC%HF` section with the exception of the FRACTION keyword. Therefore, efficiency and memory usage can be improved by reusing the HFX data from the `qs_env` and rescaling. This is now automatically done if the two HF section only differ by the FRACTION keyword (this is also true without ADMM).

New regtests for ADMM RPA@PBE were added and results for ADMM RPA@PBE0 were updated.
descripcion_limpia: pr general upgrade rpa admm used main aspects previously usage admm rpa calculations switched rirpaadmm ignored gga exchange correction simply meant hfx smaller basis rather strong approximation fixed also gw admm operations rpa rely qsenvadmmenv possible admm rpapbe arguably efficient way rpa possible xc correction thanks small refactoring general admm also means rpa specific xcsectionaux xcsectionprimary sections created stored rirpahf input section governing exx calculations cases identical dftxchf section exception fraction keyword therefore efficiency memory usage improved reusing hfx data qsenv rescaling automatically done two hf section differ fraction keyword also true without admm new regtests admm rpapbe added results admm rpapbe updated

title: Add SOS-MP2 gradients
description:
This PR adds the kernels for SOS-MP2 gradients. Common infrastructure was used as much as possible.

With this PR, the WF_CORRELATION and RI_MP2 sections change slightly to prevent doubly set sections (especially with the still to be added RPA gradients) and complicated code paths. The CPHF section and the keywords EPS_CANONICAL and FREE_HFX_BUFFER are moved into their one CANONICAL_GRADIENTS section.

For reasons of efficiency of dot products, I interchanged rows and columns of the fm_mat_S matrix used in the whole RPA code base.

title: Restore version number for development branch
description:
Essentially a rollback of #2188 because tools like AiiDA rely on the version number for dealing with output format changes.

Since we switched the versioning scheme to `<year>.<number>` and most tools assume that the version can be cast to float, there is no good way to assign development versions. Hence, we'll simply show the latest release version with the disclaimer `(Development Version)`.

See also https://github.com/aiidateam/aiida-cp2k/issues/163.
descripcion_limpia: essentially rollback tools like aiida rely version number dealing output format changes since switched versioning scheme tools assume version cast float good way assign development versions hence well simply show latest release version disclaimer development version see also

title: Refactoring of the ADMM environment
description:
This is a low-level refactoring of the ADMM environment. There were many ADMM related quantities (`aux_fit` densities, `aux_fit` KS and overlap matrices, `aux_fit` task lists, `aux_fit` MO coefficients, etc.) scattered across the code. All of these are now gathered together in the `admm_env`, itself part of the `qs_env`.

Moreover, the initialization of these quantities was also generally done all over the code. This is not the case anymore, as all these were moved to the `hfx_admm_init` routine. The same was done for the calculation of other ADMM related quantities, which were moved to new routines in the `admm_methods.F` module (`calc_admm_mo_derivatives`, `calc_admm_ovlp_forces`, `admm_projection_derivative`).

There is no visible change for the users of CP2K. This is solely an effort to improve the code.
descripcion_limpia: lowlevel refactoring admm environment many admm related quantities auxfit densities auxfit ks overlap matrices auxfit task lists auxfit mo coefficients etc scattered across code gathered together admmenv part qsenv moreover initialization quantities also generally done code case anymore moved hfxadmminit routine done calculation admm related quantities moved new routines admmmethodsf module calcadmmmoderivatives calcadmmovlpforces admmprojectionderivative visible change users cpk solely effort improve code

title: Stop the user from using non-implemented TDDFT kernels
description: There was no safeguard preventing users from defining some non-implemented TDDFT kernels. In particular, calculations using a different `HF` section in the definition of the kernel and in the main `DFT%XC` could go ahead. Since TDDFT relies on the `DFT%XC%HF` section exclusively, results would have been wrong (without warning). This is not the case anymore thanks to a `CPABORT`. Note that the newly defined `compare_hfx_sections` function might soon have other uses.
descripcion_limpia: safeguard preventing users defining nonimplemented tddft kernels particular calculations using different hf section definition kernel main dftxc could go ahead since tddft relies dftxchf section exclusively results would wrong without warning case anymore thanks cpabort note newly defined comparehfxsections function might soon uses

toolchain scripts ignore --with-mkl=no if MKLROOT variable is set for some packages
description:
As the title indicates I was trying to install the current git version of cp2k on some of our local clusters that doesn't have the `c++` bindings for mkl installed. I.e. I can only compile fortran applications with MKL and therefore I have to run `./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --install-all --with-mkl=no` to get a clean install of the toolchain libraries. That doesn't work for some libraries however, `COSMO` being the first that fails. Since I guess that cmake automatically detects my local mkl install if I have set the environment variable `MKLROOT` to point to the MKL directory.

I was eventually able to build the toolchain and cp2k successfully after unsetting this variable. So I was wondering if this is the intended behaviour, or if `--with-mkl=no` should somehow make sure that a local version of mkl is not used by accident.

title: MP2: Improve communication and memory management
description:
This PR
 - extends the idea of partial replication and blocking to open-shell systems
 - implements a reasonable automatic determination of block sizes
 - adjusts the regtests accordingly
 - changes the communication buffer to a central one to prevent OOM from too many memory (de-)allocation steps and the use of pointers to access the required parts of this buffer
 - improves the description of a few keywords

Some comments:
 - The employed memory estimates are very conservative, i.e. although we allocate a central buffer to support all required communication steps, we still consider them separately in our memory model. In that fashion, the MPI communication should not be affected too much as MPI may use its own buffers.
 - The commits will be squashed later
descripcion_limpia: pr extends idea partial replication blocking openshell systems implements reasonable automatic determination block sizes adjusts regtests accordingly changes communication buffer central one prevent oom many memory deallocation steps use pointers access required parts buffer improves description keywords comments employed memory estimates conservative ie although allocate central buffer support required communication steps still consider separately memory model fashion mpi communication affected much mpi may use buffers commits squashed later

title: fix HFX summation over neighboring cells for 1d and 2d periodic case
description:
Previous status: In case of 1d or 2d periodic systems, 4-center HFX integrals have been computed by a sum over all 3d-periodic neighboring cells. In a practical 1d- and 2d-periodic calculation, the arising issue could be circumvented by choosing a large cell size in the non-periodic direction. However, this is not practical in some cases, for example for a 2d-periodic unit cell that is large in the two periodic directions, but small in the non-periodic direction. 

Fix: Sum for 4-center HFX integrals only running over neighboring cells in periodic directions; do not sum over neighboring cells in non-periodic directions. 

Some benchmark data is collected in a github repository on the 2d-periodic MoS2 monolayer:

https://github.com/JWilhelm/MoS2_HFX_CP2K

I would be happy about comments or opinions to correct and/or further improve the PR.
descripcion_limpia: previous status case periodic systems center hfx integrals computed sum dperiodic neighboring cells practical dperiodic calculation arising issue could circumvented choosing large cell size nonperiodic direction however practical cases example dperiodic unit cell large two periodic directions small nonperiodic direction fix sum center hfx integrals running neighboring cells periodic directions sum neighboring cells nonperiodic directions benchmark data collected github repository dperiodic mos monolayer would happy comments opinions correct andor improve pr

title: Add OpenMP to most of the tensor code
description:
This PR adds OpenMP to most of the tensor code. A notable exception is `dbm_multiply` which will get its own PR.

I've added asserts to ensure that `dbm_reserve_blocks` and `dbm_iterator_start` are always called from within an OpenMP parallel region.

I would like to update the CDFT howto (https://www.cp2k.org/howto:cdft) to reflect the newly implemented Hirshfeld CDFT forces (https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/2111).

title: CDFT forces based on Hirshfeld partitioning of the electron density
description:
This update allows for CDFT force calculations to be performed with Hirshfeld partitioning of the electron density, which provides a more robust description of atomic charges than Becke partitioning. A paper based on this implementation has recently been accepted in the Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation: Ahart, C. S., Rosso, K. M. & Blumberger, J. Implementation and validation of constrained density functional theory forces in the CP2K package. Accepted (2022).

The changes are to the files: input_cp2k_dft.F, qs_cdft_types.F, qs_cdft_utils.F and qs_cdft_methods.F. I have added a new regtest for the force implementation in regtest-cdft-hirshfeld-3.
descripcion_limpia: update allows cdft force calculations performed hirshfeld partitioning electron density provides robust description atomic charges becke partitioning paper based implementation recently accepted journal chemical theory computation ahart c rosso k blumberger j implementation validation constrained density functional theory forces cpk package accepted changes files inputcpkdftf qscdfttypesf qscdftutilsf qscdftmethodsf added new regtest force implementation regtestcdfthirshfeld

title: Docker image compatibility with shifter
description:
The docker image cp2k/cp2k:9.1 has at least two compatibility issues with [shifter](https://www.nersc.gov/research-and-development/user-defined-images/) (one of the popular ways of running docker images on HPC systems).

1. The use of `--disable-shared` in the build of MPICH results in a static link of `libmpi` to the executables. This means one can not bind mount the system MPI library (relying on MPICH ABI compatibility) in order to run with MPI on multiple nodes with good performance.

2. The ENTRYPOINT method tries to write files within the image after it is created. With shifter the image is squashed making it read-only. As a work-around one could bind mount in a directory that could be written to, but the `cp2k` links are being put into non-empty directories which means a writeable overlayfs needed which shifter doesn't support. Usually `/tmp` is a writeable by default directory available in these systems. Is there a good reason to do this environment setup in the entrypoint as opposed to putting the binaries in default PATH's?

2 is probably specific to shifter and might not be an issue with other things like podman or singularity, but 1 might be an issue for other HPC container runtimes.
descripcion_limpia: docker image cpkcpk least two compatibility issues shifter one popular ways running docker images hpc systems use disableshared build mpich results static link libmpi executables means one bind mount system mpi library relying mpich abi compatibility order run mpi multiple nodes good performance entrypoint method tries write files within image created shifter image squashed making readonly workaround one could bind mount directory could written cpk links put nonempty directories means writeable overlayfs needed shifter doesnt support usually tmp writeable default directory available systems good reason environment setup entrypoint opposed putting binaries default paths probably specific shifter might issue things like podman singularity might issue hpc container runtimes

title: Fix MP2 I/O
description:
There was a problem with the I/O in GPW MP2. Each time GPW integrals are to be computed, the `prepare_gpw` routine is called on each MPI sub-communicator. This leads to PW grid info to be printed to the output, by each sub-communicator.

This was a massive mess, as the default is on sub-communicator per MPI rank. Moreover, most of the GRID info would be printed from the beginning of the output file, therefore gradually overwriting the rest of the info (especially annoying during a MD run). As a fix, I disabled I/O if the call is made from a sub-communicator. I believe no info is lost since PW_CUTOFF and other GPW parameters are printed to the output anyways.

I also added a new keyword to control the printing of DGEMM performance info to the output file. Currently, every single DGEMM call of the MP2 code leads to 3 lines being printed. This results in some 1000s of lines per MD step, which kind of pollutes the output file. The new default is to only print the average DGEMM flop rate per MPI rank, but the full output can be recovered by setting the new `PRINT_DGEMM_INFO` keyword.
descripcion_limpia: problem io gpw mp time gpw integrals computed preparegpw routine called mpi subcommunicator leads pw grid info printed output subcommunicator massive mess default subcommunicator per mpi rank moreover grid info would printed beginning output file therefore gradually overwriting rest info especially annoying md run fix disabled io call made subcommunicator believe info lost since pwcutoff gpw parameters printed output anyways also added new keyword control printing dgemm performance info output file currently every single dgemm call mp code leads lines printed results lines per md step kind pollutes output file new default print average dgemm flop rate per mpi rank full output recovered setting new printdgemminfo keyword

title: CDFT (and LDA+U) k-point sampling support?
description:
CDFT and LDA+U seem not to have k-point sampling support. Is there any source that I can learn from to understand the challenge behind it? (i.e., the challenge to generalize the Gamma-point KS potential to each k-point KS potential.)

I am especially curious about the k-point implementation in CP2K, (for example, how is the real-space density matrix obtained?)
https://aip.scitation.org/doi/10.1063/5.0007045 says:
> An extension of Eq. (9) to include a k-point sampling within the first Brillouin zone is also available in CP2K.

but left without any references. Maybe there are some unpublished developers' notes?

title: Forces evaluation for mixed force calculations is printed in the same file if you use sopt or ssmp versions
description:
Dear CP2K developers,
I noticed interesting bug:
If you use cp2k.sopt or cp2k.ssmp for mixed calculations, your forces evaluation output will be printed in the same file (*-r-1.out). It doesn't effect restart or wavefunctions printing - only messes up with output.
I detected it by running example_multiple_force_eval.py from aiida-cp2k plugin.
Best regards,
Vasyl
[multiple_force_eval.zip](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/8388405/multiple_force_eval.zip)

title: Typo in `tools/toolchain/scripts/stage5/install_elpa.sh`
description:
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/aa9c8d9962e810d991202283608a233847e566fc/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage5/install_elpa.sh#L106

Instead of
```bash
grep '\bavx512v1\b' /proc/cpuinfo 1> /dev/null && AVX512_flags+=" -mavx512v1"
```
it should be
```bash
grep '\bavx512vl\b' /proc/cpuinfo 1> /dev/null && AVX512_flags+=" -mavx512vl"
```
(Two letter `l`s were typed as number `1`s)

title: Implementation of low-scaling SOS-Laplace MP2 forces
description:
This PR brings low-scaling SOS-Laplace MP2 gradients into CP2K. The new code is an extension of the energy code for low-scaling RPA and exploits the same machinery: sparse tensor contractions. The implementation covers the following areas:
- Double hybrid (with same-spin MP2 only)
- ADMM
- Meta (tau) functionals
- Stress tensor
- Spin polarized systems
- GPW and MME for the 2-center periodic integrals and derivatives
- Libint for all other integrals
- Option to keep the Laplace quadrature constant over a MD

The code was carefully tested with many debug runs. New regtests covering all the above have been added.

title: Patching to work with MPItrampoline
description:
I'm trying to help patch CP2K so that it can work with [MPItrampoline](https://github.com/eschnett/MPItrampoline) (by @eschnett, it provides a stable MPI ABI so that you can use different implementations without recompiling the code). This requires patching of CP2K so that MPI constants are only used at runtime rather than compile time (for example).

Unfortunately,  Fortran is not my strong point and I struggled to help write the patch (a gist of the current version for CP2K 8.2 is at https://gist.github.com/ocaisa/49aa4aa6ac1bf43b36cde1716e369824).  One issue was it was unclear where exactly the initialisation of the constants should take place, we have something now that seems to work on that front but some advice on the "correct" place for this would be useful. The other problem is that some variables (like `fft_scratch_type%cart_sub_comm`) are initialised, and we seem to be failing to hack this initialisation when this type is used. description:
Hi,

I'm attempting to set up CP2K on a couple of machines, one has a 3080 and the other has an A6000 (both are  `ARCH_NUM=86`).

I can't see direct support for these in the `--gpu-ver` flag, with the closest being the A100 (`ARCH_NUM=80`).
From what I can see, the `SM_80` binary will run on `SM_86`, but won't utilise the full FP32 throughput.

Just wondered if there's something I'm missing, or if targeting the A100 is the best way forward.

I also have several laptops with `SM_75` GPUs. For these should I use V100?

Cheers,
Zach

title: Add LibXC equivalents to ADMM exchange potentials
description:
This commit adds the LibXC equivalents to the already available exchange correction functionals for ADMM. Because LibXC does not have a truncated-Coulomb-based functional (see [here](https://gitlab.com/libxc/libxc/-/issues/324)), the corresponding options still use the CP2K versions.
The main use of the new functionality is the availability of analytical 2nd derivatives (Double Hybrids, Harris functional, TDDF(P)T) with ADMM for most operators (apart from truncated Coulomb and mixed truncated Coulomb/longrange Coulomb).

Fixes #1960

ELPA GPU hangs or crashes
description:
Hi, a CSCS user on the Cray XC50 reported a problem with ELPA GPU linked by CP2K 9.1: in a test case that runs in few minutes on 6 nodes, the job hangs when starting the diagonalization with the default `ELPA_KERNEL AUTO` or it crashes with `CPASSERT failed` in `cp_fm_diag_elpa_base` when using `ELPA_KERNEL  NVIDIA_GPU` in the input file. 
The job works instead when setting manually `ELPA_KERNEL GENERIC`, therefore we have added a note on the [CP2K page of the CSCS User Portal](https://user.cscs.ch/computing/applications/cp2k): if you can access Piz Daint, the input file, the coordinate file and the Slurm submission script are in `/scratch/snx3000/lucamar/cp2k/aiida`.
Are you aware of other issues with ELPA GPU within CP2K? We have built CP2K 9.1 based on a [recent architecture file](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/arch/CRAY-XC50-gnu.psmp) with  `-D__ELPA -D__ELPA_NVIDIA_GPU` linking ELPA GPU enabled, but I have the impression that it is safer to continue linking the CPU version of the library only. Thanks for your advice.

title: Use SPLA for offloading dgemm on GPUs in the mp2 module
description:
dgemm in the mp2 module are offloaded to GPU using SPLA which already has this feature implemented. It is similar to what libsci_acc would do but it is now vendor agnostic and will support any blas vendor implementation.

- SPLA update to the latest.
- SPLA will offload the dgemm when the product 2 m n k is above a given threshold. The value is hardcoded but we can add an extra input option to set it up manually. I Fixed it to 2 * (128 ^ 3) but it can be increased further. 
- SPLA is not a hard dependency.

They are several direction for improvement. 
- Some of the matrices can be loaded only one time for instance on GPU, etc... 
- Dgemms can also be batched. But the prize to pay is code cluttering in absence of a generic interface.
- maybe use some of spla functionalities to handle mpi communication.

description:
Hello!

I tried to install cp2k 8.2.0 on my cluster, which uses CentOS 7.5, gcc 7.3.0, cmake 3.14.6, python 3.6.1, intel mpi and mkl 2018 update 3. 

I used the script to install tools:

```sh
 ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --mpi-mode=intelmpi --with-cmake=system --with-mkl=system \
--with-openblas=no --with-scalapack=no \
--with-libint=install --with-quip=install \
--with-plumed=install --with-sirius=install
```

/opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/lib/libfftw3_mpi.so: undefined reference to `fftw_ialignment_of'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
configure:17017: $? = 1
configure: failed program was:
| /* confdefs.h */

Is there some way to fix the errors I met?

Thanks

title: RI_HFX| Simplification/optimization of trace for force evaluations
description:
This is a simplification of a part of the RI-HFX force code, which also happens to bring performance benefits.

Some contributions to the forces stem from a sum over the 3-indices of 3D tensors: `F = sum_ijk A_ijk * B_ijk`

Before this PR, these contraction were handled with an intermediate 2-center tensor `T_kl = sum_ij A_ijk * B_ijl`, before the sum of the diagonal would be taken: `F = sum_kk T_kk`.

This resulted in unnecessary and expansive 3-center tensor contractions, which can be avoided by directly looping over the blocks of the two initial tensors.
descripcion_limpia: simplification part rihfx force code also happens bring performance benefits contributions forces stem sum indices tensors f sumijk aijk bijk pr contraction handled intermediate center tensor tkl sumij aijk bijl sum diagonal would taken f sumkk tkk resulted unnecessary expansive center tensor contractions avoided directly looping blocks two initial tensors

title: Protect against when GPU compilation is requested, but no PW
IR Intensities from Vibrational Analysis module
description:
This is a follow up to the recent question from the Google group: "what is the unit for IR intensity using normal mode analysis in cp2k?"

IR intensities are defined as proportional to (dmu/dQ)^2. However, CP2K prints out an intensity value in the vibrational analysis module that is |dmu/dQ| in atomic units. The same value is also printed as "intensity" in the MOLDEN vib file. I think this is at best
misleading and probably leads to errors in the post processing analysis.
I propose to change the "Intensity" output by (dmu/dQ)^2 in some often used units, e.g. km/mole as in G16.

If there are analysis programs that rely on CP2K intensity to be |dmu/dQ| we could find another solution.

title: CP2K fails with large cells and high number of OMP threads
description:
A question in the google group (https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/dfuWp6UaIOY) sparked an investigation into the behaviour of the wavelet Poisson solver. Running a calculation with a 50A x 50A x 50A cell and 36 OMP threads lead to non-physical energies and warnings by the wavelet solver. It turns out that **all** Poisson solvers yield wrong result in such conditions.

I tested the input below with the `ssmp` and `psmp`  (1 MPI rank) executables, with 16 and 32 OMP threads, for all Poisson solvers (making sure to change the periodicity when needed). Runs with 16 threads were all successful, and runs with 32 threads all failed. Reducing the box size to 40A x 40A x 40A and using 32 threads works as well. 

In the end it seems that the problem is independent the Poisson solver, contrary to the initial assumptions. I currently have no idea of what goes wrong.

```
&FORCE_EVAL                                                                                          
  METHOD Quickstep                                                                                   
  &DFT                                                                                               
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_MOLOPT                                                                 
    POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME POTENTIAL                                                                    
                                                                                                     
    &POISSON                                                                                         
      PERIODIC NONE !XYZ                                                                             
      PSOLVER WAVELET !ANALYTIC IMPLICIT MT MULTIPLOE PERIODIC                                       
    &END                                                                                             
                                                                                                     
    &XC                                                                                              
      &XC_FUNCTIONAL PBE                                                                             
      &END XC_FUNCTIONAL                                                                             
    &END XC                                                                                          
    &END DFT                                                                                         
  &SUBSYS                                                                                            
    &CELL                                                                                            
      ABC 50.0 50.0 50.0                                                                             
      PERIODIC NONE !XYZ                                                                             
    &END CELL                                                                                        
    &COORD                                                                                           
      O          0.00000        0.00000        0.11779                                               
      H          0.00000        0.75545       -0.47116                                               
      H          0.00000       -0.75545       -0.47116                                               
    &END                                                                                             
    &TOPOLOGY                                                                                        
      &CENTER_COORDINATES                                                                            
      &END CENTER_COORDINATES                                                                        
    &END TOPOLOGY                                                                                    
    &KIND H                                                                                          
      BASIS_SET SZV-MOLOPT-SR-GTH                                                                    
      POTENTIAL GTH-PBE                                                                              
    &END KIND                                                                                        
    &KIND O                                                                                          
      BASIS_SET SZV-MOLOPT-SR-GTH                                                                    
      POTENTIAL GTH-PBE                                                                              
    &END KIND                                                                                        
  &END SUBSYS                                                                                        
&END FORCE_EVAL                                                                                      
&GLOBAL                                                                                              
  PROJECT test                                                                                       
  PRINT_LEVEL MEDIUM                                                                                 
  RUN_TYPE ENERGY                                                                                    
&END GLOBAL
```

title: Dump log file on non-zero exit code
COSMA with OpenBLAS is 10x slower than ScalaPACK
description:
There seems to be a performance issue with COSMA (pdgemm) when built with OpenBLAS. Running the input file below with COSMA versus ScalaPACK exhibits a slowdown of ~10x. This is when installing CP2K with the `./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh` script and default parameters. 

The issue is most certainly related to OpenBLAS, because building COSMA with MKL (locally) or LIBSCI (on daint and eiger) leads to similar performance between COSMA and ScalaPACK.

The tests were run using the `psmp` executable and the latest CP2K git commit. The slowdown happens irrespective of the number OMP threads and MPI ranks.

Input file:

```
&GLOBAL
  PROGRAM_NAME TEST
  RUN_TYPE NONE
  &FM
    TYPE_OF_MATRIX_MULTIPLICATION COSMA !SCALAPACK
  &END
&END GLOBAL
&TEST
  &CP_FM_GEMM
    K 8512
    M 8512
    N 8512
    TRANSA FALSE
    TRANSB FALSE
    N_LOOP 1
  &END

title: Missing data-sharing clause in OMP PARALLEL region
description:
Hi,

in the file `src/qs_tensors.F` there are two `OMP PARALLEL` regions (lines 1354 and 1982). With the `DEFAULT(NONE)` clause it is necessary to specify the data shared attribute for every variable, which is accesses within the region. The variable `i` is omitted although it is referenced in both regions. once in line 1462 and once in line 2091. `i` is used in this context as an implicit loop variable, thus should be declared private, if I'm not mistaken.

title: OMP standard violation in grid_collocate_dgemm.c
description:
Hi,

the source file `src/grid/cpu/grid_collocate_dgemm.c` contains several OMP pragmas of the form `#pragma omp simd linear([list]) simdlen(X)`. In some instances the list in the `linear` clause contains pointers which are marked `const`. The first occurence is in line 221.
According to the OMP standard v5.0 section 2.19.4.6 "the *linear* clause  declares one or more list items to be private [...]". However Section 2.19.3 states in the restrictions for privatized variables "A variable that is privatized must not have a const-qualified type [...]".
Therefore, the code in `grid_collocate_dgemm.c` clearly violates the OMP standard.
This does not seem to matter for the gcc compiler, which simply accepts it, but more strict and standard compliant compilers abort with an error message at this point. Since these pointers seem to be local to the loops anyways, I don't see a reason why they need to be marked `const`.
descripcion_limpia: hi source file srcgridcpugridcollocatedgemmc contains several omp pragmas form pragma omp simd linearlist simdlenx instances list linear clause contains pointers marked const first occurence line according omp standard v section linear clause declares one list items private however section states restrictions privatized variables variable privatized must constqualified type therefore code gridcollocatedgemmc clearly violates omp standard seem matter gcc compiler simply accepts strict standard compliant compilers abort error message point since pointers seem local loops anyways dont see reason need marked const

title: remove unused variables as recognized by coding conventions on dashboard
Optionally change from accurate sum to standard sum in pw_integral_ab
description:
Hello,
This an update on the previous PR #1721 

The PR has been amended so that the standard intrinsic sum in pw_integral_ab is called optionally, with the accurate sum being the default. The standard sum is used when pw_integral_ab is called through pw_restrict or pw_prolongate (via find_coeffs). This improves the performance for qmmm calculations and the regression tests now all pass with this change. OpenMP has also been added for the standard sum.

I did not yet add the option to use the standard sum dynamically based on the number of entries but this could be added now or later on after some discussion.

Thanks,
Holly
descripcion_limpia: hello update previous pr pr amended standard intrinsic sum pwintegralab called optionally accurate sum default standard sum used pwintegralab called pwrestrict pwprolongate via findcoeffs improves performance qmmm calculations regression tests pass change openmp also added standard sum yet add option use standard sum dynamically based number entries could added later discussion thanks holly

title: Switch to new GNU arch file
Catastrophic errors in new build on ORNL Summit, last commit built 4 months ago still builds and runs fine
description:
Hi all- I am rebuilding CP2K on Summit after our OS upgrade. In August, I had a well running build using git:678222b. Now during the build, I error out with `Error: Unused variable 'p_work_tmp' declared at (1) [-Werror=unused-variable]` but can build if I remove that flag from the arch files. However, even with a totally serial, no GPU test I get rapid NaNs and infinity for all regtests I have tried. When I check out that previous commit, everything is fine and the tests pass.

Details:
GCC: I  tried 9, 10, and 7. I used 7 for the one that worked with `git:678222b`
CUDA: 11.0.3
FFTW: I used both system and local build through the toolchain. The final build that works used system
Openblas: Tried 0.3.5 (this one is built with no threading) from system, local build with toolchain, final working build used openblas/0.3.17-omp (with threads) which is the default system version
I used no other libraries, built with no packages except DBCSR
Scalapack and lapack are the netlib refs

It seems to me that it's not an issue with the libraries on Summit, based on the fact that `678222b` is fine.

arch file is this (with the `-Werror=unused-variable` flag removed from `WFLAGS` for the current version)
```
CC          = mpicc
CXX         = mpicxx
AR          = ar -r
FC          = mpif90
LD          = mpif90
#
DFLAGS      = -D__parallel -D__DBCSR_ACC -D__PW_CUDA  -D__FFTW3  -D__SCALAPACK -D__ACC 
#
WFLAGS      = -Werror=aliasing -Werror=ampersand -Werror=c-binding-type -Werror=intrinsic-shadow -Werror=intrinsics-std -Werror=line-truncation -Werror=tabs -Werror=target-lifetime -Werror=underflow -Werror=unused-but-set-variable -Werror=unused-variable -Werror=unused-dummy-argument -Werror=conversion -Werror=zerotrip -Wno-maybe-uninitialized -Wuninitialized -Wuse-without-only 
#
FCDEBFLAGS  = -fbacktrace -ffree-form -fimplicit-none -std=f2008 
CFLAGS      = -mtune=native -fno-omit-frame-pointer -O3 -funroll-loops $(PROFOPT) -fopenmp  -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror $(DFLAGS)
FCFLAGS     =  -O2 -funroll-loops  -fopenmp  $(FCDEBFLAGS) $(WFLAGS) $(DFLAGS)
#
LDFLAGS     =  $(FCFLAGS) -Wl,--enable-new-dtags -L${OLCF_FFTW_ROOT}/lib -L${OLCF_NETLIB_LAPACK_ROOT}/lib -L${OLCF_NETLIB_SCALAPACK_ROOT}/lib -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/lib64 -L${OLCF_OPENBLAS_ROOT}/lib -L'/usr/lib64' -Wl,-rpath='/usr/lib64'
LIBS     = -L${OLCF_FFTW_ROOT}/lib -lfftw3 -lfftw3_threads \
           -L${OLCF_NETLIB_LAPACK_ROOT}/lib  -llapack \
           -L${OLCF_NETLIB_SCALAPACK_ROOT}/lib -lscalapack \
           -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/lib64 -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/include -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/bin -lcudart -lcuda -lstdc++ -lnvrtc \
           -L${OLCF_OPENBLAS_ROOT}/lib -lblas -lfftw3_mpi -lfftw3 -lfftw3_omp  -lopenblas -lstdc++  -lcufft -lcublas -lrt 
#
GPUVER        = V100
OFFLOAD_CC    = nvcc
OFFLOAD_FLAGS = -g -arch sm_70 -O3 -Xcompiler='-fopenmp' --std=c++11 $(DFLAGS) 
#
FYPPFLAGS   = -n --line-marker-format=gfortran5
```

title: Suppress warning if occupation is enforced
RI-HFX| Dense contraction optimization + TDDFT forces + flavor switching
description:
Latest updates and features concerning RI-HFX:
- Some contractions involve dense tensors (especially for the forces). Those are now done with much larger block sizes, enabling better GPU acceleration
- Hybrid TDDFPT forces are now possible with RI-HFX
- Automatic RI_FLAVOR switching (from RHO to MO and vice-versa) is now possible. It is called when post-SCF calculations do not have MOs available, but the SCF was converged with the MO flavor (e.g. MP2, RPA, TDDFPT)

title: SIRIUS: wrong type of electronic structure method: FULL_POTENTIAL_LAPWLO
description:
In version 8.2 the following worked:

```
    &PARAMETER
      ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE_METHOD FULL_POTENTIAL_LAPWLO
    ...
```

with SIRIUS 7.2.7 and CP2K 4ef9989 I get now:

```
SIRIUS: run-time error

=== Fatal error at line 236 of file /tmp/software/spack-stage/spack-stage-sirius-7.2.7-2onvcagphket6ecprltlo67h3ywpxue7/spack-src/src/context/simulation_parameters.cpp ===
wrong type of electronic structure method: FULL_POTENTIAL_LAPWLO
```

and have to correct to

```
    &PARAMETER
      ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE_METHOD full_potential_lapwlo
    ...
```

... and the manual doesn't list allowed values anymore.

The second issue might already be fixed with one of the commits of @mtaillefumier in SIRIUS already.
Would be good to have a new SIRIUS release before the next CP2K release.

title: RI-HFX| Fixed a bug leading to wrongly conditioned RI basis sets
description: A bug in the RI-HFX code lead to wrongly conditioned RI basis sets. In particular, the routine `init_orb_basis_set` was called twice on the RI_HFX basis (once during the initialization of `qs_env`, once during the initialization of `ri_data`). This leads to wrong conversion matrices from primitive to spherical Gaussian functions. As a result, all RI-HFX calculations thus far have been run with basis sets slightly less accurate (and different) than the basis specified in the input file. This PR fixes this issue  and updates the regtests. The RI-HFX total energies are now typically an order of magnitude (one extra decimal point) closer to reference standard HFX calculations, as tested on the regtests and a handful of other systems.
descripcion_limpia: bug rihfx code lead wrongly conditioned ri basis sets particular routine initorbbasisset called twice rihfx basis initialization qsenv initialization ridata leads wrong conversion matrices primitive spherical gaussian functions result rihfx calculations thus far run basis sets slightly less accurate different basis specified input file pr fixes issue updates regtests rihfx total energies typically order magnitude one extra decimal point closer reference standard hfx calculations tested regtests handful systems

title: Analytical derivatives of the MO coefficients wrt nuclear coordinates and the evaluation of Atomic Polar Tensors
description:
Dear CP2K developers,

We used the code in this pull request for the calculations in this publication: [doi.org/10.1063/5.0041056](doi.org/10.1063/5.0041056).
We added another response calculation called `dcdr_linres` to `qs_linres_module`. The purpose is the analytical evaluation of the derivatives of the MO coefficients with respect to nuclear coordinates. The routine `dcdr_response_dR` calculates `$dCR = dC^0/dR^lambda_beta$`, where `lambda` enumerates the atom indices and `beta` the cartesian coordinates. The coefficient derivatives are then used to calculate the Atomic Polar Tensors (APTs) `$P^lambda_alpha,beta = d<mu_alpha>/dR^lambda_beta$`, where `mu_alpha` are the components of the electric dipole operator. The routine `apt_dR_localization` calculates the APTs using the localized dipole operator and localized orbitals.

This pull request touches quite a few files in the core of CP2K to evaluate integral derivatives with respect to nuclear coordinates - instead of electronic coordinates. I hope that I didn't incur significant performance issues in the standard ground-state calculations.

This input: https://gist.github.com/edditler/97d8a3d2b8f23eb2bb0bcbee3bacfcfc results in this output: https://gist.github.com/edditler/0b517b67bd01346f5c6ad7674c5dc21c on my machine, using the sdbg, ssmp, and psmp binaries.

I ran the regression tests using the arch files generated by the toolchain with the command `tools/regtesting/do_regtest -arch local -version psmp -maxtasks 10 -mpiranks 2 -ompthreads 2`

One can also use the implementation to evaluate the analytical Hessian matrix and improve geometry optimizations, but we didn't consider the needed interface.

Best regards,

Edward

title: Docker: Switch Gromacs to use pkgconfig
RI-HFX forces
description:
Implementation of RI-HFX forces based on sparse DBCSR tensor contractions. The new code is an extension of the work by @pseewald. New features cover:
- RI-HFX forces in the RHO and MO flavors
- Possibility of using a shorter range RI metric (for both flavors)
- Stress tensor

Additionally, the option of using RI-HFX was generalized throughout the code. It is now available for (although only in the RHO flavor):
- RPA
- MP2 (including gradients)
- TDDFPT (not including forces)
- LINRES

Some of the SCF RI-HFX routines where refactored for performance. In particular, `alloc_block_3c` now avoids allocating tons of arrays and `build_3c_integrals` was OMP parallelized.

Relevant regtests were added.

title: Enable different BD values for BMHFTD potential
No DBCSR submodule available
description:
Hi all

I tried to install cp2k 8.2 with openmpi 4.1.1, gcc 10.3 and cuda 11.4.

I had finished toolchain installation. I also cloned dbcsr to `exts/dbcsr`  and used `git submodule update --init --recursive` in the directory to check again that every package is ready.

However, when I start make cp2k, it still gave me the error:

```
Discovering programs .../opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/exts/Makefile.inc:2: *** "No DBCSR submodule available, please run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'".  Stop.
make[1]: *** [ssmp] Error 2
make: *** [all] Error 2
```

I checked the file `exts/Makefile.inc:2`, and found it checked whether `$(EXTSHOME)/dbcsr/.cp2k/Makefile` existed. The dbscr cloned from github does not have the .cp2k directory and that's why I got the error.

I tried to fix this problem: 
First, I created the .cp2k directory in `$(EXTSHOME)/dbcsr` and used cmake to generated the Makefile, and everything was fine.

Then I tried to make cp2k again. 
It gave another error:
```
Discovering programs ...
Removing stale archives for ssmp ... 
Resolving dependencies for ssmp ... 
make -C /opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/exts/dbcsr -f .cp2k/Makefile \
   INCLUDEMAKE=/opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/arch/local.ssmp \
   LIBDIR=/opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/lib/local/ssmp/exts/dbcsr \
   OBJDIR=/opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/obj/local/ssmp/exts/dbcsr \

title: Fixes for the Intel compiler related to #1664
description:
- Using the `INTENT(OUT)` attribute for `POINTER` arguments is delicate as the status of the pointer might become undefined after the routine is left. At least this happens with the Intel compiler. Therefore all `INTENT(OUT)` attributes for `POINTER` arguments have been replaced by `INTENT(INOUT)` attributes.
- Added routine name to `END SUBROUTINE` where missing
- Replaced `ENDIF` by `END IF` and `ENDDO` by `END DO`
descripcion_limpia: using intentout attribute pointer arguments delicate status pointer might become undefined routine left least happens intel compiler therefore intentout attributes pointer arguments replaced intentinout attributes added routine name end subroutine missing replaced endif end enddo end

Stress tensor for Harris functional, response solver, and subsystem DFT (embedding and atomic potential) for LDA and GGA functionals.

title: Fix thresholds
description: This PR increases the thresholds of some tests to let more Dashboard tests become green.
descripcion_limpia: pr increases thresholds tests let dashboard tests become green

title: MP2-based Double-Hybrid Gradients and Stress Tensors
description:
This PR enables Gradients and Stress Tensor for MP2-based Double-Hybrids. The SCF-part may contain
-HF (as before)
-DFT (up to GGAs with numerical or analytical 2nd derivatives)
-ADMM (some exchange corrections may require numerical 2nd derivatives)
-dispersion corrections
From the MP2-side, it contains
-additional interaction operators w/ stress tensor
-mixed metric /potential (w/o SVD)

Misc:
-Refactoring of modules and regtests directories of MP2/RPA
-Removal of old dead code
-Refactoring of Linres modules (kernel routines)

title: Feature request: support for the .magres file format
description:
Hello! 

Would it be possible to add support for outputting NMR-related quantities from cp2k into the standardised .magres file format as specified here:

https://www.ccpnc.ac.uk/output/magres/magres-format.pdf 

This would enable users to take advantage of the magres-capable post-processing tools such as [MagresView](https://www.ccpnc.ac.uk/magresview/magresview/magres_view.html) as well as being able to contribute to the [NMR database](https://www.ccpnc.ac.uk/database/). 

I hope that this would be a relatively straightforward feature to add.

Many thanks,
Kane
descripcion_limpia: hello would possible add support outputting nmrrelated quantities cpk standardised magres file format specified would enable users take advantage magrescapable postprocessing tools magresview well able contribute nmr database hope would relatively straightforward feature add many thanks kane

title: "SCF_GUESS restart" does not work with ELPA
description:
I updated cp2k to ca5cdc8c5 at two different workstations and found that
mpirun -n N cp2k ... with N>3
can not read saved wavefunctions in some cases. The code simply stops, all cores are busy and nothing happens.
If N=1,2,3 it works.
*With older versions, at least 153f2687a, it works.*
toolchain was used to update all including gcc and openmpi

psmp  version, OMP_NUM_THREADS can be different, nothing helps

I have no idea. An example of simple input with this problem is below.

`@SET Project        Li28_energy

@SET potential_path GTH_POTENTIALS

      SCF_GUESS restart

description:
We're switching to a permissive license for our newly written GPU code to make it easier for vendors to contribute. 

The vast majority of CP2K's code base will of course remain under the GPL, which we should not and cannot change.

Besides myself, the following people contributed to the new grid code: @mtaillefumier, @hfp, @mkrack, @dev-zero, @ltalirz. Could each of you please state that you agree to this license change.

title: USPEX 10.5: issue with @INCLUDE 'subsys.uspex' in CP2K
description:
Hello,

Used with CP2K, USPEX creates a file containing the cell parameters and atom positions named "subsys.uspex", which can conveniently be included in the Specific files cp2k_options_1 with the line:
  @INCLUDE 'subsys.uspex'

The problem is that the section &SUBSYS in my cp2k_options_1 already contains info about atom types like:
&SUBSYS
    &KIND C
      BASIS_SET DZVP-MOLOPT-SR-GTH
      POTENTIAL GTH-NLCC-PBE-q4
    &END KIND
&END SUBSYS
@INCLUDE 'subsys.uspex'

and the keyword &SUBSYS cannot be used more than once, whereas it is also present in subsys.uspex:
&SUBSYS
&CELL
ABC [angstrom] 4.0505422775 5.829809608 2.280013981
ALPHA_BETA_GAMMA [deg] 90 90 90
PERIODIC XYZ
&END
&COORD
SCALED
C 0.3181270957 0.5 0
C 0.6818729043 0.5 0.5
C 0.6818729043 0.5 0
C 0.3181270957 0.5 0.5
C 0.725584507 0.1219883189 0.25
C 0.274415493 0.8780116811 0.75
C 0.725584507 0.8780116811 0.75
C 0.274415493 0.1219883189 0.25
&END
&END SUBSYS

I tried to find the source file to remove the lines &SUBSYS and &END SUBSYS created by USPEX, but there is none. My guess is it's embedded in the MATLAB file, which cannot be openly modified. So I have no way to make USPEX work properly with CP2K. Besides it should rather be a 'cell.uspex' file rather than a 'subsys.uspex' file which is created by USPEX.

Please advise.

Thank you,
Xavier

title: Add Deep Potential plugin for CP2K
description:
Deep Potential (DP) is a machine learning potential based on deep neural network, first proposed by Zhang L. et al. (Doi: [10.1016/j.cpc.2018.03.016](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cpc.2018.03.016)) By learning from coordinates as well as energy and forces of different structures from DFT calculations, a potential describing the whole system could be frozen to a file. By reading the file, program could yield energy, forces etc. from structures, and then MD simulation with DFT accuracy could be fastened greatly. This PR add Deep Potential support for CP2K, with potential file (e.g. `graph.pb`) supplied, CP2K could run MD simulation. 

**Only DeePMD-kit >= 2.0.0 is supportted.**

To enable the DeePMD-kit feature, `--with-deepmd` as well as `--with-tfcc` flag in toolchain should not be set to `no`. The following ways installing has been added.

 - Please set `--deepmd-mode` to `gpu` if GPU support is needed, and load CUDA environment before installing.
 - From exist DeePMD-kit C++ interface, with`$deepmd_root` and `$tensorflow_root` (Described [here](https://docs.deepmodeling.org/projects/deepmd/en/latest/install.html#install-the-deepmd-kit-s-c-interface) in detail), by setting `--with-deepmd=$deepmd_root` and `--with-tfcc=$tensorflow_root`.
  - From source code and library from Github and Anaconda Cloud, by setting the both flags to `install`. For now, DeePMD-kit v2.0.0.b1 and Tensorflow C++ interface 2.3.0 will be installed, and only CUDA Toolkit 10.1 is OK from this way. 
  - From system, by adding the header files and library to where `ld` command could read directly.

The result from CP2K has been tested to be the same with which from Lammps, supportted by Deepmodeling.

title: Try out ELPA's new redistribution feature
description:
During yesterday's ELPA meeting it was suggested that we try its new redistribution feature. This could have several advantages:

 - we could remove our [own redistribution code](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/fm/cp_fm_diag_utils.F)
 - hopefully resolve  [#1444](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/issues/1444) as it's presumably caused by bugs in our redistribution
 - always arrive at a block size that is GPU compatible 
 - overall better performance because ELPA picks more optimal distributions

Unfortunately, I could not find any documentation on this. @marekandreas, maybe you could help?

title: Barostat issues with frozen atoms
description:
Hello,

Trying some MD runs, I have found that the coordinate scaling induced by NPT ensemble usage does affect the position of frozen atoms, and therefore, inter-atomic distances that should be fixed as well. It does not seem to be the desired behaviour in my opinion. 

I have worked on a fix for my own runs but it seems to provoke numerous failures in the test cases. My question is the following: should the npt_i and npt_f be properly fixed (which would probably imply more changes than what I did, as well as modifying some test cases), should I just implement my current version as a new keyword, or should I simply keep my version for myself ?

In any case, I will be happy to provide my working materials but my development time is limited since my PhD is about to come to an end.

Let me know which path you prefer !

Cheers,

Paul Clabaut

title: HIP grid integrate incorrect results on Mi50 (rocm 4.0.1)
description:
Hi,

We are experiencing incorrect **grid integrate** computation results on Mi50 (rocm 4.0.1), running the unit test `sample_tasks/general_tau.task`

## Environment

We are using commit b5a86d8dab03ee640791d3f22f2dca67a4a43bb1:
```
commit b5a86d8dab03ee640791d3f22f2dca67a4a43bb1
Author: Juerg Hutter <hutter@chem.uzh.ch>
Date:   Fri Jul 9 15:47:06 2021 +0200

    Coding conventions (#1581)
    
    * Coding conventions
    
    * Update DZ/DZP ADMM Basis for S/Cl
    
    * Adjust regtest tolerance
```

ROCm environment:

```
HIP version: 4.0.20496-4f163c68
clang version 12.0.0 (1100ebe275a9dcc79a9abbda902b6f10738f2f5d)
Target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
Thread model: posix
```


## How to reproduce

In `cp2k/src/grid`, we have modified `Makefile` a bit to make it work with HIP. Then we just `make` to make the two executables.

```diff

@@ -5,9 +5,10 @@ all: grid_miniapp.x grid_unittest.x

        rm -fv *.o */*.o *.x ../offload/*.o

-CFLAGS := -fopenmp -g -O3 -march=native -Wall -Wextra
-NVFLAGS := -g -O3 -lineinfo -arch sm_70 -Wno-deprecated-gpu-targets -Xcompiler "$(CFLAGS)" -D__GRID_CUDA                                                                              
-LIBS := -lm -lblas
+CFLAGS := -fopenmp -g -O3 -march=native -Wall -Wextra -D__HIP_PLATFORM_HCC__ -Wno-unused-parameter                         
+#NVFLAGS := -g -O3 -lineinfo -arch sm_70 -Wno-deprecated-gpu-targets -Xcompiler "$(CFLAGS)" -D__GRID_CUDA                                                                             
+NVFLAGS := -x hip -g -O3 $(CFLAGS) -D__GRID_HIP -D__HIP_PLATFORM_HCC__
+LIBS := -lm -lblas -llapack -llapacke -lcblas -lgfortran                                                                     

 ALL_HEADERS := $(shell find . -name "*.h") $(shell find ../offload/ -name "*.h")
 ALL_OBJECTS := ../offload/offload_buffer.o \
@@ -33,25 +34,31 @@ ALL_OBJECTS := ../offload/offload_buffer.o \
                cpu/tensor_local.o

-NVCC := $(shell which nvcc)
+NVCC := $(shell which hipcc)

-LIBS += -lcudart -lcuda -lcublas -L${CUDA_PATH}/lib64
-CFLAGS += -I${CUDA_PATH}/include -D__GRID_CUDA
+LIBS += -L$(ROCM_PATH)/lib -lamdhip64 -lrocblas -lhipblas
+CFLAGS += -I$(ROCM_PATH)/include -I$(ROCM_PATH)/hip/include -D__GRID_HIP

                gpu/grid_gpu_collocate.o \
-               gpu/grid_gpu_integrate.o
+               gpu/grid_gpu_integrate.o \
+               hip/grid_hip_context.o \
+               hip/grid_hip_collocate.o \
+               hip/grid_hip_integrate.o

 %.o: %.cu $(ALL_HEADERS)
        cd $(dir $<); $(NVCC) -c $(NVFLAGS) $(notdir $<)
+
+%.o: %.cc $(ALL_HEADERS)
+       cd $(dir $<); $(NVCC) -c $(NVFLAGS) $(notdir $<)
 endif

 %.o: %.c $(ALL_HEADERS)
        cd $(dir $<); $(CC) -c -std=c11 $(CFLAGS) $(notdir $<)

 grid_miniapp.x: grid_miniapp.o $(ALL_OBJECTS)
-       $(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)
+       $(NVCC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)

 grid_unittest.x: grid_unittest.o $(ALL_OBJECTS)
-       $(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)
+       $(NVCC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)

 #EOF
```

title: Fix DBCSR HIP build to use CC and not OFFLOAD_CC
description:
When linking with OpenBLAS that is built with g++ with OpenMP support and enabling DBCSR's HIP backend, if hipcc is used to build DBCSR's cpp files, then in the final CP2K executable, we see two OpenMP runtimes linked. One (libgomp.so) that OpenBLAS pulled in, and another (libomp.so) that DBCSR pulled in. To avoid this, it is best to build DBCSR using the same host compiler as CP2K and add a flag to indicate that the HIP_PLATFORM is AMD. This commit and an equivalent commit to DBCSR will rely on the setting of USE_ACCEL to determine which compiler and flags to use for compiling DBCSR's cpp files for AMD devices.

I tested on AMD and NVIDIA devices, but not with the OpenCL backend. Also, I am not sure if other parts of CP2K need to be modified, I only fixed INSTALL.md and the generate_arch_files.sh script. Please test and let me know if more modifications are necessary.

title: fm: Upgrade to ELPA 2021.05.001 and use GPU kernel by default
[grid] Add hip backend
description:
- Add hip backend for AMD GPU support. The same code can also be used for NVIDIA
- simple template programming used for the hip kernels. The interface is agnostic (use of template functions for cuda/hipMemcpy etc...) and the task list data structure can be reused across backend.
- cross platform compatible since there are no hardware specific (such as inline asm) code.
- algorithm are tailored for minimal use of atomic operations (not possible to avoid them for collocate) at the prize of more memory use. It means in particular that calculating the hab or the coefficients for collocate is split from the main collocate and integrate kernels. It improves occupancy and has no drawbacks in performance.
- does not assume specific device_id. If cp2k provides one then it will take it otherwise defaulting to 0 (since the backend is not mpi aware).
- Minimal changes to the Makefile (it is hacky) -> cmake
- single kernel for orthorhombic and non orthorhombic cases. Orthorhombic case consider the generic case of a orthogonal basis for the grid lattice vectors (the dh is not necessarily diagonal).  
- Performances : H2O-32.inp (benchmarks/QS) in seconds. Results taken from cp2k timings

| V100                            |                  |           |           |             |              |             |
|------------------------------ | -------------- | ------- | -------- | --------- | ---------- | --------- |
| grid_integrate_task_list |           116 | 12.3  |  4.437 |   4.437 |   4.437 |   4.437 |
| grid_collocate_task_list |           116 | 9.6  |  2.614  |  2.614  |  2.614  |  2.614 |

| Mi100                         |                   |           |             |         |            |        |
| ----------------------------- | -------------- | -------- | --------- | ------ | -------- | ------ |
|grid_integrate_task_list     |      116| 12.3 |   4.140  |  4.140  |  4.140 |   4.140|
|grid_collocate_task_list     |      116|  9.6  |  4.459  |  4.459   | 4.459  |  4.459 |
descripcion_limpia: add hip backend amd gpu support code also used nvidia simple template programming used hip kernels interface agnostic use template functions cudahipmemcpy etc task list data structure reused across backend cross platform compatible since hardware specific inline asm code algorithm tailored minimal use atomic operations possible avoid collocate prize memory use means particular calculating hab coefficients collocate split main collocate integrate kernels improves occupancy drawbacks performance assume specific deviceid cpk provides one take otherwise defaulting since backend mpi aware minimal changes makefile hacky cmake single kernel orthorhombic non orthorhombic cases orthorhombic case consider generic case orthogonal basis grid lattice vectors dh necessarily diagonal performances hoinp benchmarksqs seconds results taken cpk timings v gridintegratetasklist gridcollocatetasklist mi gridintegratetasklist gridcollocatetasklist

I'm currently running some simulations where the output does not come out with enough significant digits (position/velocity/forces), part of the reason for this is that I have chosen to output non-standard units.
Hence forces for instance comes out as:

There might be a way to do this that I just haven't found yet, but from reading the documentation I didn't find anything about this.

title: Recover line lost during cleaning of USE section
Add keyword EPS_CHECK_DIAG to global section
description:
- The keyword `EPS_CHECK_DIAG` was added to the  `&GLOBAL` section. It specifies the requested numerical accuracy for the orthonormality of the eigenvectors obtained after a diagonalization.
- A negative value for `EPS_CHECK_DIAG` disables the orthonormality check which is the default (`EPS_CHECK_DIAG = -1`)
- If the compilation flag `-D__CHECK_DIAG` has been used, the orthonormality check is always performed using the default threshold value of `5.0E-14` unless a different threshold value has been defined by the keyword `EPS_CHECK_DIAG`.
- If available, ELPA is again the default diagonalization library

title: Parallelize the calculation of 2c integrals for RI with GPW using OpenMP
Restart from calculation with &EXT_RESTART and &PERIODIC_EFIELD fails with CPASSERT
description:
When trying to use the restart file written by CP2K for a simulation with the following `EXT_RESTART` part AND a `&PERIODIC_EFIELD`:

```
&EXT_RESTART
        RESTART_FILE_NAME   ${RESFILE}-1.restart
        RESTART_COUNTERS T
        RESTART_POS T
        RESTART_BAND T
&END
```

CP2K immediately crashes with:

```
 *******************************************************************************
 *   ___                                                                       *
 *  /   \                                                                      *
 * [ABORT]                                                                     *
 *  \___/                             CPASSERT failed                          *
 *    |                                                                        *
 *  O/|                                                                        *
 * /| |                                                                        *
 * / \                                                  cp_control_utils.F:424 *
 *******************************************************************************
```

The culprit is the internal generation of the `REAL_TIME_PROPAGATION` section in case `&EXT_RESTART` is used and `RESTART_RTP` is not explicitly set the `.FALSE.`
This then triggers the assertion which checks that RTP and the periodic efield are not enabled at the same time.

The user can salvage the restart file by explicitly setting `RESTART_RTP .FALSE.`.
descripcion_limpia: trying use restart file written cpk simulation following extrestart part periodicefield extrestart restartfilename resfilerestart restartcounters restartpos restartband end cpk immediately crashes abort cpassert failed cpcontrolutilsf culprit internal generation realtimepropagation section case extrestart used restartrtp explicitly set false triggers assertion checks rtp periodic efield enabled time user salvage restart file explicitly setting restartrtp false

title: Results from periodic GW should be taken with care
description: Periodic GW calculations in CP2K (https://journals.aps.org/prb/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevB.95.235123) have recently been detected to be numerically unstable when using large, diffuse basis sets. We are working on a replacement of the current periodic GW implementation by a more reliable implementation that includes k-point sampling of the density response and the screened Coulomb interaction.

title: Feature request: auto-adjust EPS_PGF_ORB
description:
Hello devs,

When running hybrid calculations, especially with ADMM, its common to run into the following warning:

>  *** WARNING in hfx_energy_potential.F:592 :: The Kohn Sham matrix is not  ***
>  *** 100% occupied. This may result in incorrect Hartree-Fock results. Try ***
>  *** to decrease EPS_PGF_ORB and EPS_FILTER_MATRIX in the QS section. For  ***
>  *** more information see FAQ: https://www.cp2k.org/faq:hfx_eps_warning    ***

This warning comes before the HFX ERIs are calculated, and so evaluating it must be fairly quick.

I believe it would be especially useful to have an auto-converge setting for EPS_PGF_ORB. Something like `while warning exists; set EPS_PGF_ORB = EPS_PGF_ORB / 10 `. This way, you can ensure that the calculation is reasonably accurate and numerically stable (the latter being more sensitive to EPS_PGF_ORB in my experience). I have looked into the source, but having no idea of the structure of CP2K and not coded in FORTRAN before, I didn't get very far, and so I'd like to ask how hard it would be to build this into the dev branch of CP2K?

title: ELPA: memory leaks, wrong results and crashes when using small matrices
description:
as mentioned in #1428 

We currently see the following issues on our machines with ELPA:

* memory leaks (4TB RES+)
* hangs in MPI calls issued by ELPA (discovered by attaching gdb to running process and triggering a backtrace, maybe waiting for memory allocation?)
* sudden crashes of CP2K without any message (my guess is that MPI crashes, hence no processing of any I/O)
* wrong results in diagonalization

It is not related to CUDA or OpenMP (`OMP_NUM_THREADS=1` triggers it nonetheless), but most likely related to small matrices since it can be more easily triggered by small systems and when I looked into some of the hanging processes by attaching gdb and going up the stack matrix dim was < 100.

Also, while it got more problematic with the latest ELPA and CP2K releases, I observed similar problems with small matrix sizes with CP2K 7.1 (and the ELPA we used back then) but didn't have enough time to come up with a minimal reproducer to report to upstream.

title: Bump jinja2 from 2.11.2 to 2.11.3 in /tools/precommit
description:
Bumps [jinja2](https://github.com/pallets/jinja) from 2.11.2 to 2.11.3.
Release notes

Sourced from jinja2's releases.

2.11.3

This contains a fix for a speed issue with the urlize filter. urlize is likely to be called on untrusted user input. For certain inputs some of the regular expressions used to parse the text could take a very long time due to backtracking. As part of the fix, the email matching became slightly stricter. The various speedups apply to urlize in general, not just the specific input cases.

  • PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Jinja2/2.11.3/
  • Changes: https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/en/2.11.x/changelog/#version-2-11-3
Changelog

Sourced from jinja2's changelog.

Version 2.11.3

Released 2021-01-31

  • Improve the speed of the urlize filter by reducing regex backtracking. Email matching requires a word character at the start of the domain part, and only word characters in the TLD. :pr:1343

title: grid: Move tests from LIBTEST into QS/regtest-grid
call dgemv_, etc... fortran functions instead of cblas equivalent
description:
the code uses the cblas interface of MKL (openblas has it as well). When MKL is not used, then there is a fallback to hand written implementations of the functions. The problem comes from here and the only way to fix it without modifying the toolchain is to call the fortran routines with appropriate parameters. 

This fix is not portable since the fortran function mangling can be different from one compiler to another (the underscore at the end). The proper way to fix this is to use the cblas interface provided by lapack, openblas (amd BLIS also has this interface) but it means changing the toolchain so that liblapacke (that is compiled) is added to the libraries to link to *and* to add the include directory in the list of compilations options.

title: Improve OpenMP performance of collocate and integrate
Rewrite m_memory with POSIX getrusage instead of reading /proc
description:
`m_memory` is currently not thread-safe (for example when sampling memory inside a bigger OMP loop) and does not support macOS. While we could make it threadsafe, it might be a good opportunity to switch over to the POSIX API `getrusage` (same for the more extended one).

This can happen in 2 ways:

* remodel the `timeval` and `rusage` structs in Fortran and call the function directly. Advantage: no additional wrapper. Disadvantage: might break if the structs don't match what the system provides (unsure up to which part POSIX defines it) or if the datatypes don't match exactly.
* write a small wrapper in C which extracts the necessary part and returns them in a struct we define. Advantage: the C side will transparently handle order of fields, type conversion, verifies that we have a proper definition. Disadvantage: additional complexity and possible source of error (on the other hand, breakages are less likely to go unnoticed).

This is somewhat orthogonal to what #630 proposes
descripcion_limpia: mmemory currently threadsafe example sampling memory inside bigger omp loop support macos could make threadsafe might good opportunity switch posix api getrusage extended one happen ways remodel timeval rusage structs fortran call function directly advantage additional wrapper disadvantage might break structs dont match system provides unsure part posix defines datatypes dont match exactly write small wrapper c extracts necessary part returns struct define advantage c side transparently handle order fields type conversion verifies proper definition disadvantage additional complexity possible source error hand breakages less likely go unnoticed somewhat orthogonal proposes

title: Error when restarting calculations with MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL with COORD_FILE
description:
Using `EXT_RESTART` together with `MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL` and `COORD_FILE` results in a CPASSERT with a somewhat minimal error message
```
 *******************************************************************************
 *   ___                                                                       *
 *  /   \                                                                      *
 * [ABORT]                                                                     *
